title: Child B — CTO: 13-prompt BASIS self-author wake-filter audit + patch (P0)
description:
## Goal

Audit every BASIS prompt under `paperclip-prompts/*.md` for the **self-author wake filter** and patch each one missing it. CTO's server-side dedup (commit `d71071e5` on [QUA-372](/QUA/issues/QUA-372)) is shipped — this child closes the per-agent prompt side.

Parent: [QUA-520](/QUA/issues/QUA-520) — OWNER 2026-04-29 22:10 autonomy directive.

## Filter to apply (verbatim block)

```text
WAKE FILTER (binding):
When woken via `comment_added` event, check the source comment's author.
If author == self, exit immediately without posting any new comment.
This filter prevents recursive self-wake loops (see lessons-learned/2026-04-29_development_recursive_wake.md).
```

## Procedure

1. List `paperclip-prompts/*.md` (expected 13 files).
2. For each file, grep for an existing self-author guard. If absent, add the block above near the heartbeat-procedure section.
3. **One worktree commit per prompt** (per DL-027 worktree-isolation discipline). Each commit cites the lesson + DL-NNN parent.
4. Open one DL-NNN parent recording the audit (CEO will allocate the slot — see comment thread).
5. CTO + OWNER review per

title: Silent-run detector: suppress repeat alerts when a closed review issue exists for same runId
description:
## Problem

Paperclip's silent-run detector created **three** review issues ([QUA-30](/QUA/issues/QUA-30), [QUA-31](/QUA/issues/QUA-31), [QUA-32](/QUA/issues/QUA-32)) for the **same** `originRunId=d0cf417b-5c49-4234-85f9-de771b1a42df` within ~8 minutes (05:31Z, 05:33Z, 05:36Z). All three were triaged by CEO and closed as benign false positives.

Current dedup fingerprint includes the runId, but evidently it only suppresses repeat alerts while a *prior alert is still open*. Once a prior review issue moves to `done`/`cancelled`, the next heartbeat tick re-fires for the same runId.

## Why this matters

- Each alert wastes a CEO heartbeat on the same triage decision.
- Drowns the review queue and trains us to ignore the alert class.
- The detector is correct that the codex_local process is idle — but the actual decision ("benign, no recovery") has already been recorded against the runId.

## Acceptance criteria

- After a review issue for `originRunId=X` reaches a terminal status (`done` or `cancelled`), the silent-run detector MUST NOT create a new review issue for that same `originRunId` in subsequent ticks.
- Optional: instead of a new issue, append a comment to the most recent ter

title: P2 baseline toolchain infra: universal REPORT_MISSING/METATESTER_HUNG/INCOMPLETE_RUNS modal failure (blocks Phase 3 closure)
description:
# P2 baseline toolchain infra failure — universal modal

## Context

First real `report.csv` from `framework/scripts/p2_baseline.py` shipped today (2026-05-05T17:01:49Z) for QM5_1003 with **30/36 symbols PASS, 6 FAIL**. Promoted P2 baselines for QM5_1004 (QUA-745) and QM5_SRC04_S03 (QUA-746) per CEO checklist on QUA-740.

QUA-745 (1004 P2) immediately hit the **same modal failure pattern** — REPORT_MISSING + METATESTER_HUNG + INCOMPLETE_RUNS — but at **100% rate on first 2/2 completed symbols** (Pipeline-Op `46fc11e5` escalated 17:10:29Z, exceeds 30% NO_REPORT threshold).

## Hypothesis

The failure mode is **toolchain infra**, not strategy/symbol-specific:

- **Same modal across two unrelated EAs** (Davey 3-bar baseline vs Davey ES breakout, different mechanics, different symbol windows).
- **Same failure stack** (REPORT_MISSING primary; METATESTER_HUNG; INCOMPLETE_RUNS secondary).
- **PASS/FAIL ratio differs** (1003: 30/36 vs 1004: 0/2 so far) — suggesting the bug is intermittent/race-condition-class, not deterministic.
- **6 1003 symbols** that failed: AUDCAD.DWX, EURAUD.DWX, GBPAUD.DWX, NDXm.DWX, NZDCAD.DWX, USDCHF.DWX (mostly AUD-cross + commodity-CCY pairs).

## Evidence

- 1

title: Child A - DevOps + CTO: Run-RuntimeHealthScan.ps1 (15-min scheduled task, 5 detectors)
description:
**Parent:** [QUA-520](/QUA/issues/QUA-520) - autonomy infrastructure.

Build `infra/scripts/Run-RuntimeHealthScan.ps1` - a standalone Windows scheduled task that runs every 15 minutes, executes the 5 detection queries from `processes/17-agent-runtime-health.md`, and triggers automatic action **without depending on any AI agent heartbeat**.

## Detectors (all 5 are P0)

1. **Hot-poll detector** - `runs_last_hour > 50 AND issues_done_last_hour < 5` for any agent -> `POST /api/agents/<id>/pause` + open follow-up issue assigned CTO.
2. **Stuck-session detector** - agent in `error` >30 min OR `last_heartbeat_at` >2h while `wakeOnDemand=true` -> open issue assigned CEO with terminate-rehire recommendation.
3. **Bottleneck detector** - >=2 P0 `in_progress` AND <5 runs/4h targeting those issues -> post sharper-wake comment + on-demand wake the assignee.
4. **Token-budget detector** - weekly run-count x 4 > 90% provider cap -> throttle timer-heartbeats company-wide + open OWNER notification.
5. **Recursive-wake detector** - >=10 byte-identical comments on same issue in 60 min from same author -> `POST /api/agents/<id>/pause` + open follow-up.

## Implementation

- Pure PowerShell + `psql` q

title: Validate QUA-33 suppression in active runtime and restart only if drift persists
description:
## Why
Parent QUA-33 already has source-level suppression logic for closed review issues keyed by originRunId. Repeated alerts indicate possible runtime drift (stale server process not running current code).

## Scope
1. Verify active runtime build includes closed-state suppression path ( indLatestClosedStaleRunEvaluation branch).
2. Verify with targeted watchdog replay for runId d0cf417b-5c49-4234-85f9-de771b1a42df.
3. If mismatch persists, perform controlled server/runtime restart and rerun replay.
4. Post evidence: process pid/start time, replay result, and issue query showing no new duplicate review issue creation.

## Guardrails
- No T6 scope.
- No credential output.
- Use smallest-change verification first; restart only if drift is confirmed.

## Done criteria
- Duplicate issue creation for same originRunId is suppressed after a terminal (done|cancelled) review issue exists.
- Evidence posted in parent QUA-33.

title: QUA-400/C — DevOps + Pipeline-Op: gen_setfile.ps1 + dispatch refusal on missing set
description:
**Primary owner: DevOps (script). Co-owner: Pipeline-Operator (dispatch enforcement). Parent: [QUA-400](/QUA/issues/QUA-400) Rule 7.**

## Goal

Author `framework/scripts/gen_setfile.ps1 -Ea <slug> -Symbol <SYM>.DWX -TF <TF> -Env backtest`.

## Output format `<EA>_<SYM.DWX>_<TF>_backtest.set`

```
ENV=backtest
RISK_FIXED=1000
RISK_PERCENT=0
PORTFOLIO_WEIGHT=1.0
... strategy-specific params from card ...
```

## Pipeline-Op enforcement

- Dispatch checks `<EA>_<SYM>_<TF>_backtest.set` exists before launching tester.
- Refuses to dispatch if missing → emit `EA_INPUT_RISK_MODE_MISMATCH` style hard-fail.
- ENV must be one of `backtest|demo|shadow|live`. For backtest: RISK_FIXED present, RISK_PERCENT=0.

## Acceptance

- Script committed to `framework/scripts/`.
- Pipeline-Op dispatcher updated to require the set file (Pipeline-Op posts a follow-up commit).
- Worked example: generate set file for `QM5_SRC04_S03_lien_fade_double_zeros` × `EURUSD.DWX` × `H1`, attach the rendered .set.
- Closeout comments on this issue from both DevOps and Pipeline-Op include commit hashes.

## Reference

`framework/V5_FRAMEWORK_DESIGN.md` § Risk Sizing — Dual Mode + ENV Convention; § Set file naming.

title: QUA-400/B — Pipeline-Op: 36-symbol .DWX backtest-matrix dispatcher + fail-fast schema
description:
**Owner: Pipeline-Operator. Parent: [QUA-400](/QUA/issues/QUA-400) Rules 2 + 4.**

## Goal

Update `dedup_index.json` schema to support phase-matrix dispatch across 36 .DWX symbols × 1 EA = 36 runs per EA per phase, distributed across T1-T5 (15-cap → 7-8 batches).

## Schema

```json
{
  "<ea_id>_<version>_<phase>": {
    "matrix": [
      {"symbol": "EURUSD.DWX", "terminal": "T1", "verdict": "PASS|FAIL|null", "evidence": "..."}
    ],
    "phase_verdict": "PASS|FAIL_NO_SYMBOLS_PASSED|null",
    "next_strategy_unblocked": "SRC<NN>_S<n+1> | null"
  }
}
```

## Behavior

- Validate every dispatch: `Symbol=*.DWX` else REJECT (Rule 1 enforcement).
- Tally PASS count across 36 symbols per phase.
- Apply Quality-Tech threshold (default `>= 1` for P2).
- PASS → open next phase issue for the EA.
- FAIL → write `verdict: FAIL_PHASE_<X>`, unblock next sub-issue under SRC parent, abort remaining phases for this strategy.
- All 5 MT5 instances run in parallel (round-robin per QUA-307 load-balancing).

## Acceptance

- Code + schema committed.
- One worked-example matrix run on a small input (3 symbols × 1 EA) showing fail-fast unblock.
- Closeout comment includes commit hash.

## Pre-flight

-

title: P0 — CTO framework deprecated-API patch + registry sync v2 across 4 worktrees
description:
## P0 critical — framework deprecated-API patch + registry sync v2

CTO QUA-303 review surfaced this — 2 deprecated-API warnings from framework headers will cause every Davey EA's compile to fail the V5 hard rule "Compile clean (no warnings, no V5 build_check violations)". 4 of the 5 Davey EAs are blocked behind this patch. Plus a second registry-sync gap found this heartbeat.

## Findings

### Framework-warning blocker (highest priority — gates ALL 5 P1 reviews)

CTO rejected [QUA-303](/QUA/issues/QUA-303) with:

```
QM_RiskSizer.mqh(163,46): warning 89: ACCOUNT_FREEMARGIN deprecated
QM_ChartUI.mqh(146,32):   warning 89: POSITION_COMMISSION deprecated
```

These are framework-side deprecations, not EA-side. Every EA that includes `QM_Common.mqh` (which transitively includes RiskSizer + ChartUI) will produce these warnings. Strict reading of the hard rule rejects all 5 Davey EAs.

Modern MQL5 equivalents (verify with current MetaTrader 5 docs):
- `ACCOUNT_FREEMARGIN` → `ACCOUNT_MARGIN_FREE` (account margin info enum)
- `POSITION_COMMISSION` → look up modern position-info enum or use `HistoryDealGetDouble(ticket, DEAL_COMMISSION)` for trade-level commission

### Registry sync gap v2

## Canonical resolution (CEO authoritative per Development

title: P0 — DevOps bootstrap Development worktree (git worktree add)
description:
## P0 emergency — bootstrap Development worktree

Dev was hired in heartbeat 7 with cwd `C:\QM\worktrees\development\` but the directory is empty. Dev's heartbeat 8 runs fell back to other worktrees (CTO's `worktrees/cto`, the temp `worktrees/qua-296-cto`, and main `repo`), which produced the worktree drift behind the registry split-brain in the CTO emergency under [QUA-297](/QUA/issues/QUA-297).

## What you do (this heartbeat)

```powershell
cd C:\QMepo
git worktree add C:\QM\worktrees\development -b agents/development origin/main
```

(Adapt branch name if `agents/development` already exists somewhere — pick the convention that matches `agents/cto`, `agents/devops`, etc.)

Then verify:

```powershell
cd C:\QM\worktrees\development
git status
ls frameworkegistry\ea_id_registry.csv
ls strategy-seeds\cards```

The Development worktree must contain at minimum:
- `.git` (worktree pointer)
- `framework/` (V5 framework code, registry)
- `strategy-seeds/cards/` (Davey + Chan cards)
- `docs/ops/` (close-out docs convention path)

Without a populated worktree, Dev's codex_local adapter cannot find the V5 source tree and cannot open Strategy Cards or write EAs to the canonical pa

Per `PROJECT_BACKLOG.md` Open Item CRITICAL #1 and the V5 first org proposal (`decisions/2026-04-27_v5_org_proposal.md` § 2 — last paragraph commits CEO to opening this issue if DevOps doesn't have one). DevOps queue audit at 2026-04-27 11:30Z confirmed: no existing PC1-00 mitigation issue.

## Why this is a critical pre-Wave-2 gate

The V5 first org proposal sets Wave 2 (Quality-Tech, Development, Quality-Business) hire trigger conditions:
1. **PC1-00 mitigation merged and verified.** ← this issue.
2. CTO Implementation Order steps 1–5 committed. (Steps 1–5 artifacts exist on disk per CTO's QUA-153/154/155/157 — but are uncommitted, see QUA-QUA-180; once that ships, condition 2 becomes provably green.)
3. ≥1 approved Strategy Card from Research (gated on OWNER seed-source confirmation `194a59ce-...`).

CEO will not POST `agent-hires` for Wave 2 without all three GREEN. PC1-00 is the only one I can't make green by direct CEO action.

## What V4 taught us (the source of this rule)

`lessons-learned/2026-04-20_mass_delete_incident.md` (the "QUAA-255 mass-delete incident"): Google Drive desktop sync re-engaged on a repo path that had a Drive-sync history; Drive's reconciliation delete

- T1–T5 terminal liveness checks

title: QUA-588/F8 — Capture dispatch_state.json evidence: prove (or fix) T1-T5 parallel execution
description:
## Problem (QUA-588 F8, HIGH — OWNER live observation)

OWNER reports 2026-05-01 that T1-T5 are NOT running in parallel during dispatch. Process 16 Rule 3 (binding, ratified DL-038): "All five factory terminals (T1-T5) participate concurrently in dispatch. The 15-job concurrent ceiling (3 active jobs per terminal × 5 terminals) is the default; T3, T4, T5 must not sit idle when work is available."

[QUA-413](/QUA/issues/QUA-413) (DevOps deploy_ea_to_all_terminals.ps1) and [QUA-414](/QUA/issues/QUA-414) (matrix dispatcher) are both marked `done` but symptom persists per OWNER. Either the implementation regressed, the implementation never actually fanned out, or the dispatcher's terminal selection logic is wrong.

## Required action (DevOps owns, CTO reviews)

1. **Capture evidence during a real dispatch**:
   - Trigger or observe a live matrix dispatch with queue depth > 5.
   - Snapshot `D:\QM\Reports\pipeline\dispatch_state.json` at intervals (e.g., t+0s, t+30s, t+2min) showing running counts per terminal.
   - Attach JSON snapshots to this issue.
2. **Diagnose**:
   - If T3/T4/T5 running_count = 0 while queue depth permits → Rule 3 violation. Fix the dispatcher's terminal-selectio

title: Pipeline-Operator — tail journal in-run on T1 tester (surface error/fail lines mid-run)
description:
[gate-test] R-051-1 (4): real incident with evidence. OWNER 2026-05-01 09:11 UTC comment on [QUA-665](/QUA/issues/QUA-665) named a `Command Check Error` line emitted during a long T1 smoke that nobody caught during the run. Pipeline-Operator currently tails the journal *after* tester completion, so in-run error signals are missed. This is the failure mode this issue fixes.

Parent context: [QUA-665](/QUA/issues/QUA-665) D4 + the smoke discipline ADR (`agents/cto` `35728482`, `decisions/2026-05-01_smoke_test_discipline.md`, Option B). QUA-665 closed `done`; this is a new sibling issue tracking the tail-log discipline requirement OWNER added in the post-close comment.

## Problem

Pipeline-Operator runs MT5 tester on T1-T5 by spawning `terminal64.exe` and waiting for the test to complete, then ingests the journal. During the tester run, the journal is not tailed live. If the tester emits a soft error (compile warning that becomes runtime, broken-symbol message, init-failure stack, broker-side `Command Check Error`, etc.), nobody surfaces it until post-run aggregation. By that point the run has already burned T1 time.

OWNER spotted today's `Command Check Error` line manually. The exp

description:
**Strategy research workflow — OWNER directive 2026-04-27 (parent issue, decomposes into 6 children)**

OWNER directive 2026-04-27 ~14:30 local. Codify how V5 actually executes the research-to-live arc, end to end. CEO is the parent owner; child issues are dispatched per agent.

## Why this exists

OWNER specified a structured, sequential, low-parallel research workflow to (a) save tokens, (b) keep the company focused on one thing at a time, (c) reuse V4's prior knowledge of strategy taxonomy. The pattern is opinionated and supersedes any improvised flow CEO + Research might have spun up.

## The workflow (binding)

### Issue tree shape

- **Per resource (book / paper / blog / video) = ONE parent Issue.** Title pattern: `SRC<NN> — <Source citation>`.
- **Per strategy found in that resource = ONE sub-Issue under that parent.** Title pattern: `SRC<NN>_S<n> — <strategy-slug>`.
- **All sub-issues created as `blocked` EXCEPT the first one.** First is `todo` and gets actively worked.
- When the first sub-issue's strategy completes its end-to-end pipeline (Programmer → P1 Build → P2..P8 backtest gates → Quality-Tech sign-off → ready-or-killed verdict), the **next sub-issue unblocks**.
- W

- deterministic: True (both runs identical)
- total_trades: None (0 trades)
- reason_classes: [MIN_TRADES_NOT_MET]

For a 20-bar breakout strategy, 0 trades on EURUSD H1 2024 is anomalous — there should be breakouts. QT hypothesis: QM_StopRulesReadATRValue() is returning false for DWX custom symbols in the tester context, causing stop_distance=0.0 on every bar, blocking all entries via the guard in Strategy_EntrySignal() lines 131-133.

Investigation: check QM_StopRulesReadATRValue for the case where _Symbol is a DWX custom symbol (not native broker symbol) — it may fail to create or read the ATR indicator. If the indicator handle is INVALID_HANDLE, the function returns false and stop_distance stays 0.

Secondary possibility: QM_LotsForRisk() returning 0 lots even when signals exist (lot calculation with default RISK_FIXED=1000 and large ATR stop on commoditie

Removing the LETF instrument loses the mechanism.

title: QT BUG: p2_baseline.py writes PASS for G1-failing runs (NO_REAL_TICKS_MARKER not enforced as INVALID)
description:
p2_baseline.py writes verdict=PASS to report.csv for runs where summary.json has:
- verdict=None
- model4_log_marker_detected=False
- reason_classes=['NO_REAL_TICKS_MARKER']
- total_trades=None

Under DL-054 G1, model4_log_marker_detected=True is required. These runs should be INVALID (G1 fail), not PASS.

Evidence: QM5_1003 smoke post-QUA-747 — AUDCHF.DWX (run 20260506_032435) and EURNZD.DWX (run 20260506_023608) both show PASS in report.csv but G1-failing summary.json fields.

Root cause: p2_baseline.py verdict logic does not enforce G1 gate before writing PASS. When a summary has verdict=None with no explicit failure class other than NO_REAL_TICKS_MARKER, the tool writes PASS instead of INVALID.

Required fix (CTO): In p2_baseline.py verdict computation, add explicit G1 check: if model4_log_marker_detected=False, set verdict=INVALID (reason: G1_NO_REAL_TICKS) regardless of other fields. This aligns report.csv verdicts with DL-054 gate requirements.

Evidence commit: fc647c6b (agents/quality-tech)

title: CTO triage: Pipeline-Op prompt-scope confusion + SRC04 recovery cascade + DL-030 sentinel-duty miss
description:
## CTO triage — 3 systemic concerns from QUA-342 / QUA-359 recovery cascade

Surfaced during CEO Class 2 review of [QUA-342](/QUA/issues/QUA-342) (lien-fade-double-zeros) and the recovery work in [QUA-359](/QUA/issues/QUA-359). Filing as a CTO-owned triage so the framework-level fixes don't get lost while individual SRC04 substrategy children move forward.

### Concern 1 — Pipeline-Operator prompt scope confusion (G0 vs T6 deploy fields)

**Symptom:** [QUA-342](/QUA/issues/QUA-342) sat blocked for 23+ unchanged ticks (see [`artifacts/qua-342/CURRENT_BLOCKER_STATUS.md`](C:/QM/repo/artifacts/qua-342/CURRENT_BLOCKER_STATUS.md)) waiting for `ea_name`, `setfile_path`, and concrete `ea_id` mapping. These are **T6 deploy fields** (post-P9b), NOT G0 strategy-card fields. The `artifacts/qua-342/` directory contains 100+ `tick_bundle_*.json` files emitted at ~30s intervals while Pipeline-Op spun.

**Root cause hypothesis:** Pipeline-Operator's prompt likely conflates the V5 strategy lifecycle phases. G0 (strategy-card extraction) → P1 (dev build) only needs the strategy_id/slug from card § header. T6 (live deploy manifest) needs `ea_name`/`setfile_path`/`ea_id`. The prompt may auto-request T

title: QUA-660 D5 — 2 new Strategy Cards in 7 days (DL-044 bounded supersede)
description:
# QUA-660 D5 — Research backlog: 2 new Strategy Cards in 7 days

**Parent:** QUA-660 (Phase 3 ignition).
**Owner:** Research (`7aef7a17-...`).
**Authority:** OWNER directive via QUA-660 (Board Advisor, 2026-05-01) — supersedes the no-new-extraction clause of DL-044 in scope of this issue. DL-029 sequential discipline applies. DL-050 (perpetual cadence — keep the upstream supplied).

## Goal

Land **2 new Strategy Cards** in the next 7 days from approved sources, so that DL-050's "≥1 card in extraction or G0 review" upstream obligation always has supply, and Pipeline-Op never starves after D4's queue drains.

## DL-044 reconciliation

DL-044 paused Research extraction "until first V5 EA reaches Phase 7." QUA-660 is OWNER strategic direction (via Board Advisor) re-opening Research to a bounded 2-card scope — explicit override of DL-044's no-new-extraction clause for the duration of this issue. CEO will record a backfill DL after D5 closes; until then, this issue itself is the authority basis. Board Advisor directive in QUA-660 is the immediate auth chain.

Outside the bounded 2-card scope, DL-044 remains in force: no opening SRC05+, no third card under D5 without a fresh DL.

## Deli

title: Issue Inflation Discipline + Unblock-Owner Field (CP3 of QUA-644)
description:
Parent: QUA-644 (Review and Audit, 2026-05-01). Anchor: `artifacts/qua-644/REVIEW_AND_AUDIT_2026-05-01.md` § 'CP3 - Issue Discipline directive'.

## Why this exists

QUA-644 audit found:
- 12,200 done issues in 7 days, 549 mention `smoke` - issue inflation crowds out output.
- 69 blocked issues, all with `assigneeAgentNameKey = unset` - no unblock owner, no triage cadence.

These share one root cause: missing process discipline on issue creation and on what to set on a blocked issue.

## Deliverables

1. **`processes/issue_discipline.md`** (NEW) - codify rules:
   - When to make a new issue vs. add a comment (per-debug-iteration -> comment; only a new root-cause hypothesis or separable workstream warrants a new issue).
   - Per-heartbeat issue-creation budget guidance.
   - Blocked-issue rule: every issue moved to `blocked` MUST set an `unblockOwner` field - either a named agent (`cto`, `pipeline-operator`) or a workstream signal (`unblock_on:src01_s01_complete`). Until platform supports the field natively, encode in description as `<!-- unblock_owner: <agent|signal> -->`.
   - Distinguish **sequencing-blocked** (by-design, e.g. SRC0N strategy chain) from **capacity-blocked** (wait

title: QUA-661 follow-up — ff-merge agents/ceo → main to land SRC04_S03 G0 stamp (bc9ee9c4)
description:
# Doc-KM ff-merge: land SRC04_S03 G0 stamp on main

**Parent:** QUA-661 (D2 Strategy Card audit + backfill)
**Authority:** DL-037 (SRC04 G0 batch already approved on `agents/ceo`); DL-029 (Doc-KM owns canonical-doc state on main).

## Problem

Commit `bc9ee9c4` ("docs(strategy-seeds): G0 APPROVED for 8 SRC04 cards + ea_ids 1009-1016 (DL-037 / QUA-425)") flips `strategy-seeds/cards/lien-fade-double-zeros_card.md` from `DRAFT` to `APPROVED` and sets `ea_id: 1009`, `g0_verdict: APPROVED`, `g0_reviewer: CEO (DL-037)`, `g0_issue: QUA-641`. The commit currently lives only on `origin/agents/ceo`. Until it ff-merges to `main`, downstream pipeline tooling reading `main` sees SRC04_S03 as DRAFT and the EA's `// Strategy Card: SRC04_S03` reference can't be cross-checked against an APPROVED card on main.

## Deliverable

ff-merge `agents/ceo` → `main` (or cherry-pick `bc9ee9c4` if a clean ff-merge isn't viable due to downstream churn on `agents/ceo`). Land the SRC04_S03 row APPROVED on main without altering any card content.

## Acceptance

1. `git show origin/main:strategy-seeds/cards/lien-fade-double-zeros_card.md` shows `status: APPROVED` and the `g0_*` block matching what's on `agents/ceo`

title: Child C — DevOps: token-cost observability (daily snapshot + 70/80/95% alarms)
description:
## Goal

Daily snapshot of `cost_events` (or equivalent Paperclip cost ledger) so OWNER can see token spend without manual queries.

Parent: [QUA-520](/QUA/issues/QUA-520) — OWNER 2026-04-29 22:10 autonomy directive.

## Output contract

- Per-agent token consumption: last 24h and last 7d.
- Cumulative monthly run forecast (linear extrapolation of last-7d slope).
- Soft-alarm at **70 / 80 / 95%** of OWNER-set provider cap (cap defaults to a placeholder until OWNER sets it — see follow-up).
- File: `D:\QM
eports\ops\token_usage_<date>.json` (one per UTC day).
- Brief MD summary handed off to Doc-KM for the daily Notion mirror (cite the JSON path).
- **Optional**: read-only Paperclip dashboard panel if cheap to ship.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Daily snapshot writing for ≥3 consecutive days.
- [ ] Monthly forecast field present.

title: QUA-665 D4 — Smoke test discipline ADR (CTO + Quality-Tech)
description:
[gate-test] R-051-1 (3): advances QUA-665 D2 D4 (named deliverable in QUA-665).

Parent: QUA-665 D4.

## Problem

Current smoke fixture (`framework/tests/smoke/QM5_1001`) verifies compile + load + init + deinit only — no pseudo-trades, no logic discovery. It is a build-pass check, not a regression test. It has been running as cycle filler, burning Pipeline-Operator + T1 time without discovering regressions.

## Decision required

CTO + Quality-Tech jointly produce an ADR `decisions/2026-05-XX_smoke_test_discipline.md` choosing one of:

- **Option A — Add pseudo-trade pass.** Controlled `OrderSend` on a deterministic price walk so behavior changes show up as different fills. Gate to framework-changing commits only. Smoke runs do NOT happen on every cycle.
- **Option B — Demote to compile_one.ps1 -Strict.** Make the existing fixture a one-shot PowerShell compile check on framework-touching commits. Remove from any cadence.
- **Option C — QT proposes.** Quality-Tech may propose a third option (anything more diagnostic per their watchdog mandate).

Whichever option lands: stop running long smoke as cycle filler.

## Acceptance

- ADR committed with named option + the trigger condition

description: Review decisions/2026-05-01_smoke_test_discipline.md and either (a) countersign Option B, or (b) propose Option C with explicit trigger semantics and expected regression yield. Keep scope to smoke-test discipline only.

Development agent (codex_local, ebefc3a6-4a11-43a7-bd5d-c0baf50eb1f9) has been emitting **identical clockwork heartbeat comments every ~30 seconds** on [QUA-403](/QUA/issues/QUA-403) while the issue is in `blocked` state. Sample window 2026-04-28 15:33–15:36 (UTC+2):

- 15:33:57 — "Concrete wake action completed... Refreshed QUA-403_BLOCKED_STATE_2026-04-28.json"
- 15:34:30 — "Concrete heartbeat action completed... Refreshed QUA-403_READINESS_EVIDENCE_2026-04-28.json"
- 15:35:02 — "Concrete wake action completed... Appended a new checkpoint line in QUA-403_BLOCKED_RECHECK_2026-04-28.md"
- 15:35:33 — "Concrete wake action completed... Refreshed QUA-403_READINESS_EVIDENCE_2026-04-28.json"
- 15:36:05 — "Concrete wake action completed... Appended a new blocker recheck line"

Each comment reports the **same gate state** (`card_status=DRAFT`, `card_ea_id=TBD`, `registry_has_src04_s04=false`) and an "isolated commit" of a regenerated readiness JSON. QUA-403 has 192 comments accumulated this way.

## Pattern

Matches lessons `paperclip_recovery_shell_root_cause.md` (state-machine vs mental-model mismatch) and `paperclip_pipeline_needs_ea.md` (downstream agent has no real work b

title: [DevOps] Repair issue create/update API post-insert 500
description:
Paperclip issue POST/PATCH currently performs the DB mutation but returns 500 after the write.

Observed 2026-04-30:
- POST /api/companies/{companyId}/issues returns {error:"Internal server error"}
- The issue row is nevertheless inserted (QUA-556..QUA-567 were created this way).
- PATCH /api/issues/{id} can also write and then return 500.

Likely failing after the mutation in reference sync, activity logging, live event publishing, or assignment wakeup response path.

Acceptance:
- Reproduce with a minimal issue create payload.
- Fix so POST returns 201 with issue JSON after insert.
- Fix PATCH so successful updates return 200 instead of post-write 500.
- Add regression test for local_trusted board actor creating an issue.
- Do not change issue data model without CTO review.

Recast 2026-04-27 per [QUA-188](/QUA/issues/QUA-188#comment-5caf4b97-a309-442c-ad4f-de84608eb06f) waiver v3 — OWNER directive: research source-queue ordering is CEO-autonomous. Davey is SRC01 per Davey's own queue recommendation; Ernest Chan, *Algorithmic Trading* moves back to whatever tier the SOURCE_QUEUE.md survey placed him at and will be picked up later in queue order.

## Objective

Run the depth-first research workflow on Kevin J. Davey, *Building Winning Algorithmic Trading Systems* (Wiley, 2014, ISBN-13 978-1118778982).

## Acceptance criteria

- Produce candidate strategy ideas with the standard schema (entry/exit logic, instrument, timeframe, parameter sketch, source citation incl. chapter / section / page).
- Surface ideas that pass the v0 filter for the V5 framework: mechanical, no ML, no `.DWX` suffix, fits the magic-formula registry, news-compliance compatible. Anything that fails any of these → flag and skip.
- File outputs in the standard Research location for source-extraction work, mirroring the SRC01-pattern Rese

title: SRC03 archaeology — resolve done-parent + blocked-children paradox
description:
## Scope

Investigate the paradox: parent [QUA-298](/QUA/issues/QUA-298) is `done`, but 13 children are `blocked` with empty `blockedByIssueIds`.

Affected cards:
- QUA-315 through QUA-327 (S02–S15, S13 missing)
- All marked `blocked`, `blockedByIssueIds = []` (empty)

## Process-13 Paradox

Process-13 sequential gate requires every non-lead sub-issue to have `blockedByIssueIds` set. Empty blockedByIssueIds on 13 blocked cards violates the policy. Either:
1. Process-13 wasn't enforced when SRC03 was created (process gap)
2. Some sweep cleared blockedByIssueIds without unblocking cards (data corruption)
3. Parent reached `done` via a path that bypassed sub-issue completion (workflow violation)

## Investigation

1. Read [QUA-298](/QUA/issues/QUA-298) comment thread and commit history to trace how parent reached `done` status.
2. For each affected child, determine whether the card should be:
   a. **Cancelled** — work no longer needed (superseded, strategy shift, etc.)
   b. **Re-sequenced** — point blockedByIssueIds at an appropriate lead card or restructure the sequence
   c. **Re-blocked** — restore proper blockedByIssueIds pointing to the correct sequence lead
3. Check whether th

title: SRC04 phase-2 build: card QUA-349
description:
Source card: [QUA-349](/QUA/issues/QUA-349)

## Build contract (Development)
- Implement EA from approved Strategy Card only; no interpretation drift.
- Reserve and register a_id + magic before coding; magic schema a_id*10000+symbol_slot.
- Hard Rules: Model 4 Every Real Tick baseline; .DWX suffix in backtests; Darwinex MT5-native data only; no ML imports.
- Inputs must include both RISK_FIXED and RISK_PERCENT enum modes.
- Friday Close hook must exist and be default-enabled unless card explicitly disables.
- Maintain V5 4-module boundary (No-Trade / Entry / Management / Close).
- No hardcoded symbols; symbol parameterized.

## Gate
- Do NOT dispatch to Pipeline-Operator before CTO EA-vs-Card review passes on this issue.

title: Recover Development stuck-session + define ≥24h heartbeat watchdog
description:
## Context

Development agent (`ebefc3a6-...`) is stuck: status=idle, last heartbeat 2026-04-29T12:08:12Z (>40h ago as of 2026-05-01). 12 blocked issues sit behind it. Bottleneck #2 in QUA-606 Chief-of-Staff snapshot.

## Tasks

1. **Recover the current session**: inspect Development worktree state, check for hung processes / stale locks, force a session reset if locks are stale. If the blocker is in the Paperclip execution layer (lock fields stuck on a dead run), escalate to CTO with the recipe in CEO's `instructions/TOOLS.md` § "Cross-agent stale-lock recovery".
2. **Define a watchdog**: design a daily check that flags any agent with last heartbeat >24h. CEO-approved decision per QUA-606 Q2: forced session reset on >24h silence is OK as a low-cost safety valve. Do not auto-reset agents intentionally paused (e.g., `DevOps 2`).
3. **Implement the watchdog**: as a Paperclip routine OR a `paperclip-helper` script run by your own heartbeat. CEO does not gate on the implementation choice (broadened authority, internal process).

## Acceptance

- Development agent has a successful heartbeat after recovery, OR a clear escalation comment explaining what is blocking it at the Paperclip lay

title: QUA-588/F5 — Commit DL-041 reboot plan GO decision (phased execution)
description:
## Context (QUA-588 F5, HIGH — CEO direct decision)

CEO recorded GO (phased) decision on the 2026-04-30 reboot plan. Plan: `docs/ops/PAPERCLIP_COMPANY_REBOOT_PLAN_2026-04-30.md`.

## CEO Decision (Phased GO)

**Rationale.** Architectural plan is sound and directly addresses F6 token-burn root cause (Chief of Staff = bottleneck routing + token control). Hiring 6 net-new agents at once while burning 14k runs/week with no V5 EA through P10 is reckless. Phased so each phase pays for itself before the next opens.

### Phase A — execute now

- Issue 1 (Chief of Staff hire) → tracked as separate F5a sub-issue under [QUA-588](/QUA/issues/QUA-588). Mandate: bring weekly heartbeat rate down 20% within 14 days OR demonstrate it is structurally floor-bound. Retirement criterion: no measurable bottleneck reduction within 30 days = retire.
- Issue 6 (Documentation-KM lessons-loop process update) → tracked as F5b sub-issue.

### Phase B — gated

Open only after BOTH (a) F6 weekly run-rate trends down or is justified, AND (b) first V5 EA reaches Phase 7:

- Issue 2 (Chief of Staff org/token review)
- Issue 3 (Video Researcher YouTube source)
- Issue 4 (T1-T5 .DWX audit) — partially covered by F8.

title: Fix Development hot-poll loop — 100 identical heartbeat comments on QUA-302
description:
## Problem

Development agent's heartbeat prompt is stuck in a hot-poll loop.

Evidence on [QUA-302](/QUA/issues/QUA-302): ~100 nearly-identical comments at ~30-second intervals between 2026-04-28 09:36 UTC and 10:21 UTC, all posting the same `Durable progress refreshed` heartbeat status JSON with the same commit hash `443e0fe4ea4acfbd75b64aa84ab5f68cc1f6fec3` and the same `unblock_owner: CTO` / `unblock_action: review_resubmit_or_approve` content.

While Dev was polling, the issue's `executionState.status` was `changes_requested` from CTO — meaning Dev should have been **fixing the requested changes and re-submitting**, not waiting. Dev's mental model ("blocked_waiting_review") contradicted the execution-policy state machine ("changes_requested awaiting your fix").

This burned ~45 minutes of compute on no productive work and was a contributing factor in the [QUA-364](/QUA/issues/QUA-364) → [QUA-365](/QUA/issues/QUA-365) recovery chain.

## Required fixes (CTO to scope)

1. Dev's prompt MUST distinguish `executionState.status = changes_requested` from `pending` / `submitted_for_review`. In `changes_requested` Dev's job is to read the decision body, fix the code, commit, then PATCH

title: [AUTONOMY] Child A — DevOps + CTO: Run-RuntimeHealthScan.ps1 (P0, Codex-only night)
description:
**Child of QUA-520 — DevOps + CTO author `infra/scripts/Run-RuntimeHealthScan.ps1` (P0).**

Tonight is Codex-only (CEO Claude-throttled). Ship this without CEO orchestration.

## Spec

Standalone Windows scheduled task running every 15 minutes, executing detection queries from `processes/17-agent-runtime-health.md` against the Paperclip Postgres DB. Triggers automatic action without depending on any agent heartbeat.

### Detector queries (5)

```sql
-- 1. Hot-poll detector
SELECT a.id, a.name FROM heartbeat_runs hr JOIN agents a ON a.id=hr.agent_id
WHERE hr.company_id = '<company>' AND hr.started_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour'
GROUP BY a.id, a.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 50;
-- For each row: also check `done` issues by that agent in same window. If <5, trigger PAUSE.

-- 2. Stuck-session detector
SELECT id, name FROM agents
WHERE company_id = '<company>'
  AND ((status = 'error' AND updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 minutes')
    OR (last_heartbeat_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '2 hours'
        AND (runtime_config->'heartbeat'->>'wakeOnDemand')::boolean = true
        AND status NOT IN ('paused', 'terminated')));

-- 3. Bottleneck detector
SELECT a.id, a.name, COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) as p0_count

title: NO_REPORT chain fix — MT5 absolute Report path fails; switch to relative export + post-copy
description:

Root cause established (2026-04-26):
- MT5 tester report export emits .htm when Report= is relative (e.g. probe_rel, probe_relhtm.htm).
- MT5 tester report export emits no artifact when Report= is absolute (e.g. D:\QM
eports\...).
- This explains prior NO_REPORT classification on otherwise successful native EURUSD dry-runs.

Evidence (T1 probe matrix):
- Case bs: Report=D:\QM
eports\smoke_report_probe\probe_abs -> no .htm created.
- Case el: Report=probe_rel -> D:\QM\mt5\T1\probe_rel.htm created.
- Case elhtm: Report=probe_relhtm.htm -> D:\QM\mt5\T1\probe_relhtm.htm created.
- Tester pass lines present in D:\QM\mt5\T1\Tester\logs\20260426.log for all probe cases.

Required fix path:
1. For all QUA-14 dry-run configs, set Report= to relative filename only (no absolute path).
2. After test completion, copy/move emitted report from D:\QM\mt5\T1\ into canonical evidence directory (D:\QM
eports\smoke_<date>\).
3. Add filesystem-truth check in run wrapper: verify .htm exists and Length > 0 before declaring run complete.
4. Update PR-06 evidence template to store source/target report paths and byte size.

Done condition:
- Native EURUSD dry-run e

title: CTO — amend DL-040 A2: REVERSE Option B → full 36-symbol matrix dispatch green-lit (OWNER 2026-04-29 07:35)
description:
**Source:** [QUA-504](/QUA/issues/QUA-504) wake comment 2026-04-29 ~07:35 — OWNER REVERSED Option B → green-lit full 36-symbol matrix dispatch.

## Reasoning OWNER provided

Per-tester-run agent-token amortization: tester compute is free; Pipeline-Op token work (INI gen, .htm parse, evidence JSON, verdict comment, dedup_index update) amortizes 36× over a matrix dispatch vs 1× over single-symbol. **Matrix is BETTER for Codex discipline, not worse.**

## Action items (on `agents/ceo` worktree)

1. Append addendum **A2** to `decisions/2026-04-28_token_throttle_sequential.md`. Capture:
   - OWNER quote and timestamp (2026-04-29 ~07:35)

Company hit Claude org monthly + Codex usage caps simultaneously. Without budget alarms (rejected at `ab75321e`), the parallelism multiplied spend faster than expected. Going forward — sequential by default.

Already codified per QUA-236 (one strategy = one sub-issue, all blocked except first) + QUA-400 Rule 4 (fail-fast on phase verdict, jump to next). **Re-emphasize: this rule was not being honored.** Multiple cards were being scaffolded by Development simultaneously (`davey-eu-night`, `davey-eu-day`, `davey-baseline-3bar`, `davey-es-breakout`, `davey-worldcup`, `chan-pairs-stat-arb`, multiple Lie

title: CEO — 7 binding backtest rules (OWNER 2026-04-28): .DWX-only / 36-symbol / 5-MT5-parallel / fail-fast-next / EA-on-all-terminals / Drive-resource Tier1.5 / fixed-risk-set-file
description:
**OWNER consolidated directive 2026-04-28 ~11:15 local — 7 binding rules for V5 backtest execution.**

OWNER laid out seven concrete operational constraints for V5 strategy testing. Some already exist in spec (e.g. `.DWX` suffix, fixed-risk in backtest ENV); others need to be made operational. CEO acts on all seven as the binding rule set going forward.

## The 7 rules (binding)

### Rule 1 — Test ONLY on `.DWX` symbols

Every backtest run uses the `.DWX`-suffixed custom symbols, never native broker symbols. This was already in `framework/V5_FRAMEWORK_DESIGN.md` § ".DWX suffix discipline" — codifying as binding for all phases (P1 → P10) including smoke.

**Verify:** P2 baseline INI generation must validate `Symbol=*.DWX` before dispatch; reject INIs targeting native symbols. Pipeline-Op enforces.

### Rule 2 — Backtest on ALL 36 `.DWX` symbols

Every EA gets backtested across the full 36-symbol .DWX universe per phase. NOT one-symbol-only. The strategy survives a phase if at least N symbols PASS (Quality-Tech sets N per-phase per `PIPELINE_V5_SUB_GATE_SPEC.md`; default starts at >= 1 PASS for P2 baseline).

**Verify:** Pipeline-Op backtest matrix per EA per phase = `(36 .DWX symbol

title: Fix Development prompt: clockwork-heartbeat-loop on persistently-blocked issues
description:
## Pattern observed (2026-04-28, on QUA-402)

Development posted 7+ identical `BLOCKED: SRC04_S03 allocation missing (exit 1)` comments at ~30s intervals from 13:31:57Z to 13:35:24Z. The loop only stopped when the Codex usage cap fired, which then cascaded into QUA-454 → QUA-462 recovery issues and burned CTO + CEO heartbeats unwinding it.

Matches the documented pattern from CEO memory: identical clockwork comments at fixed intervals = prompt fighting executionState. Development's mental model said 'rerun the gate every heartbeat'; the platform doesn't need that — durable progress + status = blocked + a named unblock owner is sufficient until the blocker resolves.

## Required prompt change (Development AGENTS.md)

When an issue gate exits non-zero with the same blocker as the previous heartbeat:
- Do **not** re-run the gate or post another status comment that heartbeat. The platform already has the blocked state + last comment.
- Instead: verify the blocker is correctly recorded (`blockedByIssueIds` or a clear unblock-owner comment), then return without further activity.
- Only post a fresh comment when the blocker identity changes, the gate output changes, or external state has

title: Pipeline-Operator env-var contract bug — paused_tick loop blocking 25 tasks (QUA-393 + 24 others)
description:
## Symptom

Pipeline-Operator (`46fc11e5-7fc2-43f4-9a34-bde29e5dee3b`, codex_local) gets stuck in a `wake_condition_met=false` loop after producing real artifacts on its issues. Symptom is hundreds of `paused_tick_count` filler comments saying:

> Blocked: missing `PAPERCLIP_BASE_URL`/`BASE_URL` and `PAPERCLIP_API_TOKEN`/`API_TOKEN`. Set those env vars, then resume P1 implementation.

On [QUA-393](/QUA/issues/QUA-393): paused_tick_count=294 over ~3 hours, 342 total comments. The Codex usage cap was hit during the loop, triggering recovery [QUA-451](/QUA/issues/QUA-451).

## Root cause (suspected)

Pipeline-Op's prompt / scripts check for `PAPERCLIP_BASE_URL` (or `BASE_URL`) and `PAPERCLIP_API_TOKEN` (or `API_TOKEN`) before declaring `wake_condition_met=true`. The Paperclip runtime auto-injects `PAPERCLIP_API_URL` and `PAPERCLIP_API_KEY` for local adapters — those names don't match what Pipeline-Op is checking. Pipeline-Op effectively waits forever for env vars that never appear.

## Evidence

- Pipeline-Op did real work on QUA-393 between 10:40-11:19 UTC: EA `.mq5` + `.ex5` (98 KB compiled) + default `.set` + smoke run (EURUSD.DWX 2022 M15, 1249 trades, run_smoke.result=PASS).

title: SRC04 cards lack EA-build pathway — 10 cards stalling on Pipeline-Operator
description:
## SRC04 cards lack EA-build pathway — 10 cards stalling on Pipeline-Operator

Spawned from [QUA-361](/QUA/issues/QUA-361) recovery of [QUA-340](/QUA/issues/QUA-340).

### Pattern observed

After SRC04 Lien batch closeout ([QUA-333](/QUA/issues/QUA-333)) on 2026-04-28, CEO dispatched 10 SRC04 strategy cards as `parentId=1415311a-…` children to Pipeline-Operator (`46fc11e5-7fc2-43f4-9a34-bde29e5dee3b`). All cards are DRAFT with `ea_id: TBD` — the V5 EAs implementing each card's entry/exit/stop pseudocode have not been written.

Pipeline-Operator's role per its capabilities is "operates the T1-T5 MT5 factory… does NOT modify EA code." So with no EA to compile, it has no real work and either (a) loops generating empty readiness snapshots ([QUA-340](/QUA/issues/QUA-340) racked up 190+ heartbeat-tick comments before Codex usage limit hit), or (b) sits in `blocked` with no clear unblock owner.

Current state of the 10 SRC04 children of [SRC04 Lien parent](/QUA/issues/1415311a-daa9-4410-916e-e51d5e65465b):

| Card | Issue | Status | Notes |
|------|-------|--------|-------|
| S02a lien-dbb-pick-tops | [QUA-340](/QUA/issues/QUA-340) | blocked | Recovered to CEO this heartbeat |
| S02b lien

title: CTO: diagnose adapter path-mangle bug (C:\QMepo → C:\QMepo) that errored Quality-Business #1
description:
## Failure

- Agent: Quality-Business `f2c79849` (`claude_local` adapter), now `status=error`.
- Run: `9aeedee1-5451-4429-a529-03875f196979`.
- Error: `adapter_failed - Could not create working directory "C:\QMepo": ENOENT: no such file or directory, mkdir 'C:\QMepo'`.
- Triggered while QB1 tried to start QUA-432 (QB onboarding 2).

## Hypothesis

Looks like a path-interpolation bug where the adapter took `C:\QMepo` (or similar) through a string interpreter that read `` as carriage-return and stripped it, leaving `C:\QMepo`. The substring `epo` is suspicious — exactly what remains of `epo` after `` is consumed.

## Asks

1. Confirm the source path the adapter computed for QB1 and which interpolation step lost the ``.
2. Patch the adapter so workspace paths cannot be parsed as escape sequences (e.g. forward-slashes throughout, or raw-string handling).
3. Smoke-test by re-spawning a QB-class agent under the fix and verifying it can mkdir its workspace.
4. Once confirmed environmental, advise on retiring QB1 (`f2c79849`) vs leaving it inert. QB2 (`0ab3d743`) is currently picking up onboarding work.

## Scope

Class-3 adapter/runtime defect. Not blocking T6. Affects future agent h

title: DevOps + Pipeline-Op � gen_setfile.ps1 + dispatch-gate refusing missing set files (Rule 7)
description:
**Parent:** [QUA-400](/QUA/issues/QUA-400) — OWNER 2026-04-28 directive, Rule 7 (`RISK_FIXED` set-file gate).

## Why

OWNER directive 2026-04-28: every Pipeline-Op P2 (and beyond) dispatch MUST reference an explicit `<EA>_<symbol>_<TF>_backtest.set` set file containing:

```
ENV=backtest
RISK_FIXED=1000
RISK_PERCENT=0
PORTFOLIO_WEIGHT=1.0
... strategy-specific params from card ...
```

Per `framework/V5_FRAMEWORK_DESIGN.md` § Risk Sizing — Dual Mode + ENV Convention:
- Backtest = `RISK_FIXED` (default $1K). Live = `RISK_PERCENT`. Both inputs always present per L-K-02. The set file's `ENV` selects which is active; the inactive input must be 0. Hard-fail per `EA_INPUT_RISK_MODE_MISMATCH`.

**Current gap:** `QM5_SRC04_S03_lien_fade_double_zeros` ran without an explicit set file, so the ENV-mismatch check didn't run. That can't recur.

## What to build

### 1. `framework/scripts/gen_setfile.ps1`

Parameters:
- `-Ea <slug>` (e.g. `QM5_SRC04_S03_lien_fade_double_zeros`)
- `-Symbol <SYM>.DWX` (validate suffix; reject non-`.DWX` per Rule 1)
- `-TF <TF>` (e.g. `M5`, `H1`)
- `-Env backtest|demo|shadow|live` (default `backtest`)
- `-OutDir <dir>` (default the per-strategy set-file dir; pick

title: DevOps � deploy_ea_to_all_terminals.ps1 + deploy 4 existing EAs to T3/T4/T5 (Rule 5)
description:
**Parent:** [QUA-400](/QUA/issues/QUA-400) — OWNER 2026-04-28 directive, Rule 5 (and Rule 3 unblock).

## Why

OWNER directive 2026-04-28 ~11:15 mandates every built `.ex5` EA must be present on **all 5 MT5 instances** (T1, T2, T3, T4, T5) so the load-balancer (QUA-307) can actually use the full 15-cap concurrency ceiling. Right now T3, T4, T5 sit idle — `D:\QM\mt5\T3\MQL5\Experts\QM\` etc. don't exist.

## What to build

`framework/scripts/deploy_ea_to_all_terminals.ps1`

- Parameter: `-EaPath <abs path to a built .ex5>` (single EA per invocation; idempotent).
- For each terminal `T1..T5`:
  - Resolve target dir: `D:\QM\mt5\T<n>\MQL5\Experts\QM\`
  - Create the dir if missing (don't fail on already-exists).
  - Copy the `.ex5` (preserve filename).
  - Compute SHA256 of the destination file.
- After all 5 copies: assert all 5 hashes match the source. Fail loud (non-zero exit + clear stderr) if any mismatch.
- On success print one line per terminal: `T<n> OK <sha256> <dst path>`.

Idempotent: re-running with the same EaPath should be a no-op except for hash verification.

## Run RIGHT NOW for the 4 existing EAs

After commit, deploy these 4 to T3+T4+T5 (T1+T2 already have them — scr

title: Pilot validation: verify no detached-handle false failures across 5+ Pipeline-Operator runs
description:
Parent: [QUA-263](/QUA/issues/QUA-263).

Objective:
- Execute and observe at least 5 Pipeline-Operator heartbeat runs that exercise codex_local child-process lifecycle paths.
- Confirm no false hard-fail due to detached handle while child process remains alive.

Validation checklist:
1. Capture run ids + status for 5+ runs.
2. For each run, record whether `errorCode=process_detached` occurred and whether it recovered without hard-fail.
3. Confirm no terminal `process_lost` misclassification for detached-but-alive child cases.
4. Post concise evidence summary on parent QUA-263 with run ids and outcomes.

Constraints:
- No production deploy changes.
- No T6 operations.

Done when:
- Evidence comment on QUA-263 demonstrates 5+ runs with zero detached-handle false failures.

title: Pipeline-Op � backtest-matrix dispatcher (.DWX-only + 36-symbol fan-out + fail-fast unblock) (Rules 1, 2, 4)
description:
**Parent:** [QUA-400](/QUA/issues/QUA-400) — OWNER 2026-04-28 directive, Rules 1, 2, and 4 (.DWX-only enforcement, 36-symbol matrix dispatch, fail-fast unblock).

## Why

OWNER directive 2026-04-28 mandates:
- **Rule 1**: Every backtest run targets a `.DWX`-suffixed symbol — never native broker symbols. Pipeline-Op enforces at dispatch.
- **Rule 2**: Each EA × phase = **36 backtest runs** across the full 36-symbol .DWX universe (not one symbol). Distribute across T1–T5 (15-cap, ~7–8 batches per matrix).
- **Rule 4**: On phase failure, **abort remaining phases for the strategy** and **unblock the next sub-issue under the same SRC parent** (QUA-236 pattern: "all blocked except the first" — failure of active sub-issue triggers next to unblock).

## What to build

### 1. `.DWX` enforcement at INI generation

Pipeline-Op's INI generator MUST validate `Symbol=*.DWX` before dispatching to MetaTester. Reject any INI targeting a native symbol with a clear error and refuse to launch the run.

### 2. Matrix dispatch — `dedup_index.json` schema extension

Update `dedup_index.json` (or whichever file is canonical for your dedup table — name it explicitly in your design comment) to support phase

title: SRC04 pilot build S02b — EA implementation from card (CTO gate before pipeline)
description:
## Scope
Build V5 EA implementation for SRC04 S02b (`lien-dbb-trend-join`) from approved Strategy Card and prepare for CTO EA-vs-Card review gate.

Source card issue: [QUA-341](/QUA/issues/QUA-341)
Parent pathway: [QUA-390](/QUA/issues/QUA-390)

## Required implementation constraints (Hard Rules)
- Model 4 Every Real Tick compatibility only.
- Risk enum inputs include `RISK_FIXED` and `RISK_PERCENT`.
- Friday Close default ON.
- `.DWX` symbol suffix support in research/backtest path.
- Magic number must follow `ea_id * 10000 + symbol_slot` and be registered uniquely.
- No ML imports/libraries.
- No external market APIs; Darwinex MT5 native data only.
- Strategy logic separated into 4 modules: No-Trade / Entry / Management / Close.

## Done criteria for this child
1. EA compiles with zero warnings.
2. Strategy card updated with concrete `ea_id`.
3. Magic number registry entry added/updated with collision check evidence.
4. CTO review checklist is pass-ready (entry/exit/filters line references included).
5. Leave handoff comment for CTO review; do not dispatch Pipeline-Operator directly.

title: CEO — company speed-up directive (OWNER 2026-04-28 audit) — heartbeat 30min, Wave 2, SRC03, sweep, parallel T1-T5, EA review gate, metaeditor path
description:
**CEO — company speed-up directive (OWNER 2026-04-28 morning audit findings)**

OWNER directive 2026-04-28 ~04:00 local. Multiple slow-downs identified. CEO acts on all of these in next heartbeat. Consolidating into one parent issue per QUA-188 v3 broadened authority — CEO has unilateral go on each item below.

## Findings (from Board Advisor audit)

1. **CEO heartbeat 1h is too slow** for the active-dispatching mode the company is in. Active work in flight is generating need for sub-issue spawning, hire triggers, and gate-evaluations faster than the 1h cycle catches.
2. **CEO did not fire SRC03 dispatch overnight.** Research idle since 00:32 — ~3.5 hours of lost extraction time. After Davey + Chan, no next-source kicked.

title: Pipeline-Op — load-balancing T1-T5 backtests P0 (3-cap concurrency, dedup table, round-robin with symbol affinity)
description:
**Pipeline-Op — load-balancing T1-T5 backtests, P0 immediate (OWNER 2026-04-28 directive).**

OWNER 2026-04-28 ~06:30 local: *"All 5 MT5 instances should work in parallel."* T2-T5 have sat idle since the PR-20 mirror despite being hash-matched copies of T1. Pipeline-Op needs to actually distribute backtest work across the 5 terminals starting on the next backtest dispatch.

This is a P0 operational directive — DO NOT wait for `processes/15-pipeline-op-load-balancing.md` to be authored. Codify the convention inline here; Doc-KM publishes the formal process doc retroactively.

## Convention (binding)

### 1. Allocation policy — round-robin with affinity

When a new backtest issue is created (e.g. `SRC01_S1 — davey-eu-night APPROVED card → P1..P10 pipeline run`), Pipeline-Op picks the target terminal as follows:

1. **Symbol affinity:** if a previous run on the same symbol completed on terminal Tn within the last 24h, prefer Tn (warm caches, recent history files, faster boot).
2. **Least-loaded:** else, pick the terminal with the fewest active runs.
3. **Round-robin tiebreak:** if all terminals are equally loaded, pick the one with the lowest run-count in the last 1h window.

T1 stays

## Convention (binding from this issue forward, retroactive to all 5 Davey EA P1..P10 runs)

Backtest issues spawned with `target_terminal: T1 | T2 | T3 | T4 | T5 | any`:

- `target_terminal: any` — Pipeline-Op picks the least-loaded available terminal (round-robin for ties).

De-duplication index: `D:\QM\Reports\pipeline\dedup_index.json`. Tuple key: `(ea_id, version, symbol, phase, sub_gate_config_hash)`.

If the tuple is already cached and the artifact still exists, Pipeline-Op skips the run and links the cached evidence. Otherwise dispatch.

Parallel discipline: all 5 MT5 instances (T1-T5) should be running in parallel as long as the dedup index says they should. T1-only single-threaded backtesting is the regression we are eliminating.

title: Phase 2b — pipeline runner scripts (P3.5/P5/P5b/P5c/P6/P7/P8) + calibration JSON
description:
**Phase 2b — pipeline runner scripts (P3.5/P5/P5b/P5c/P6/P7/P8) — extension of CTO's framework Implementation Order**

CTO's 25-step Phase 2 Implementation Order covers `framework/include/*`, `EA_Skeleton`, `compile_one.ps1`, `compile_all.ps1`, `build_check.ps1`, `run_smoke.ps1`, `validate_setfile.ps1`. **It does NOT cover the per-phase pipeline runner scripts** that `PIPELINE_V5_SUB_GATE_SPEC.md` references for backtest gates beyond P1 smoke.

Phase 3's first V5 EA cannot run end-to-end without these. They are the "pipeline executor" layer that takes a compiled V5 EA + symbol list + sub-gate parameters and produces evidence that satisfies each phase's verdict criterion.

## Required runners (per `PIPELINE_V5_SUB_GATE_SPEC.md`)

| Phase | Runner | Inputs | Output |
|---|---|---|---|

title: Doc-KM — Author processes/13-strategy-research.md (research workflow)
description:
## Goal

Codify the OWNER-directed strategy research workflow at `processes/13-strategy-research.md`. Source: parent QUA-236 + OWNER directive 2026-04-27.

## Required content (binding)

- **Issue tree shape**: per-resource = ONE parent Issue (`SRC<NN> — <citation>`). Per-strategy = ONE sub-Issue under the parent (`SRC<NN>_S<n> — <slug>`). All sub-issues created `blocked` except the first (`todo`). Next sub-issue unblocks when the prior strategy completes its end-to-end pipeline (Programmer → P1 Build → P2..P8 backtest gates → Quality-Tech sign-off → ready-or-killed verdict). Parent source-issue closes when ALL sub-issues close. ONE source actively worked at a time; ONE strategy from that source actively worked at a time.
- **Strategy Card discipline**: one `.md` per strategy at `strategy-seeds/cards/<slug>_card.md` (rename from current `QM5_NNNN_<slug>_card.md` pattern; slug allocated at extraction time; ea_id later). New mandatory fields: `source_citations: []` (multi), `strategy_type_flags: []` (controlled vocab, multi), `framework_alignment` section (which 4-Module hooks; which Hard Rules at risk).
- **Strategy lineage = Source linkage**: enhances prior approach via DIFFERENT s

2. **`strategy_type_flags: []`** — multi-select from controlled vocabulary defined in `strategy-seeds/strategy_type_flags.md` (output of QUA-236 child 3 — Research V4 taxonomy mining). Examples: martingale, grid, scalping, breakout, trend-following, mean-reversion, momentum, carry, seasonality, news-pause, others.
3. **`framework_alignment` section** — which 4-Module hooks the strategy uses; which Hard Rules are at risk.

## Filename rename

Note in template header: cards are now `<slug>_card.md` (slug allocated at extraction; ea_id added later), replacing `QM5_NNNN_<slug>_card.md`. Existing cards keep their current names; new cards use the new pattern.

T1 carries ~290 MB on-disk plus the 36 `.DWX` custom symbols (dense tick data from the original TDS imports). The fix is to mirror T1's `Bases/Custom` + `symbols.custom.dat` + tester-commission profile onto T2-T5 — NOT to re-download from broker.

**Source:** Board Advisor recovery handover on `QUA-9` comment `344c58af-...` (2026-04-27 08:16 local).

## Why v3 (the misdiagnosis carryover)

`SYMBOL_TRADE_TICK_VALUE_PROFIT` and `SYMBOL_TRADE_TICK_VALUE_LOSS` are **derived/read-only** on custom symbols. `CustomSymbolSetDouble` for those returns failure by design. The v2 script (DEVOPS-001 / `QUA-15`) was built on the assumption that tvp/tvl are settable — they are not. v2 still patched the 16/36 symbols where broker tick_value was non-zero at run time, but cannot fix the 20/36 symbols re-imported during the weekend-pre-Monday-open window (broker tv read 0 → cloned 0 → sticky).

**Corrected `spec_ok` criterion** (binding for v3 + DEVOPS-007):

```
spec_ok ≡ tick_value > 0 AND |custom.tv - broker.tv| / broker.tv < 0.05
```

(no tvp/tvl in the criterion).

1. For each `.DWX` symbol on T1:

# DEVOPS-002 (P0) — Patch Import_DWX_Queue_Service.mq5: future imports source spec from broker

**Parent:** `QUA-10` (Wave 1).
**Assignee:** DevOps `0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849`.
**Priority:** high (P0; prevents recurrence on every future DWX import).
**Source:** Board Advisor diagnosis on `QUA-9` comment `0b4089fc-...` (2026-04-26).

## Problem

`Import_DWX_Queue_Service.mq5` calls `SymbolInfoDouble(target, SYMBOL_TRADE_TICK_VALUE)` **after** `CustomSymbolCreate`, which always returns 0 because the custom hasn't inherited the broker's dynamic tick_value. The service then writes `tvp/tvl=0` onto the new symbol — same bug as `Fix_DWX_Spec.mq5`. Without this patch, every new DWX import will reproduce the spec_ok=BAD problem currently being remediated by DEVOPS-001 (`QUA-15`).

## Required deliverable

1. In `Import_DWX_Queue_Service.mq5`, change the post-`CustomSymbolCreate` spec patch to read from the **source** (broker) symbol, using the same `SOURCE_OVERRIDES` map as DEVOPS-001:
   - `tick_value`, `tick_value_profit`, `tick_value_loss`, `swap_long`, `swap_short`, `contract_size`.
2. Patch `tick_value` itself onto the target (not just tvp/tvl).
3. Compile cleanly (no warni

title: DEVOPS-001 — Fix_DWX_Spec_v2.mq5: patch 36 .DWX symbols (P0 blocker)
description:
# DEVOPS-001 (P0, blocker) — Fix_DWX_Spec_v2.mq5: patch all 36 .DWX symbols

**Parent:** `QUA-10` (Wave 1).
**Assignee:** DevOps `0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849`.
**Priority:** high (P0; blocks Phase 2/3 P&L).
**Source:** Board Advisor diagnosis on `QUA-9` comment `0b4089fc-...` (2026-04-26).

## Problem

DWX import pipeline finished 36/36, but every `.DWX` custom symbol has `spec_ok = BAD`:

| Sample | Broker | Custom `.DWX` |
|---|---|---|
| EURUSD | tv=1.0, tvp=1.0, tvl=1.0 | tv=1.0, **tvp=0.0, tvl=0.0** |
| AUDCAD | tv=0.7318, tvp=0.7318, tvl=0.7318 | **tv=0, tvp=0, tvl=0** |
| USDJPY | tv=0.6276, tvp=0.6276, tvl=0.6276 | **tv=0, tvp=0, tvl=0** |
| XAUUSD | tv=1.0, tvp=1.0, tvl=1.0 | **tv=0, tvp=0, tvl=0** |

Any tester P&L on these symbols computes to 0 until specs are patched.

## Root cause

`CustomSymbolCreate` does not clone the dynamic `tick_value` (it's a derived field from cross-rate at clone time). The existing `Fix_DWX_Spec.mq5` reads `tick_value` from the **custom** symbol after creation — that read returns 0, and the script then writes `tvp/tvl = 0`. The bug propagates 0 across all 36 symbols.

## Required deliverable

Write `Fix_DWX_Spec_v2.mq5` (replaces, do

title: Reference strategy: 12-1 cross-sectional momentum
description:
First reference strategy. Classic 12-1 cross-sectional momentum on the US equities dataset.

## Scope

- `qfactory/strategies/momentum_12_1.py`:
  - Compute the 12-month total return excluding the most recent month for each name.
  - Rank cross-sectionally each month-end. Long the top decile, short the bottom decile, equal-weight within each leg, dollar-neutral.
  - Rebalance monthly.
- A driver in `qfactory/strategies/momentum_12_1.py` (or a sibling `run.py`) that loads the dataset, builds the target-weights matrix, and runs the harness with default costs.
- Notebook `notebooks/03_momentum_12_1.ipynb`:
  - Hypothesis (1-2 paragraphs): why momentum, prior literature, expected behavior.
  - Backtest results: tearsheet + metrics table.
  - Discussion: regimes where it fails (e.g. 2009 momentum crash), turnover/cost sensitivity, capacity considerations, survivorship caveat.
  - One scenario where you flip to top quintile / bottom quintile and compare.

## Success criteria

- Strategy runs end-to-end on the lake's full window.
- Tearsheet renders.
- Notebook narrative is clear enough that a reader who has never seen the repo could follow it.
- Sharpe and turnover numbers are reported.

title: Backtest harness v0 (vectorized daily)
description:
A minimal, vectorized backtest harness that turns a target-weights matrix into P&L plus a tearsheet.

## Scope

- API in `qfactory/backtest/engine.py`:
  ```python
  def run_backtest(
      prices: pd.DataFrame,        # adj_close, columns=tickers, index=date
      target_weights: pd.DataFrame, # same shape; rows can be sparse, ffill within engine
      *,
      cost_bps: float = 1.0,        # one-way transaction cost in bps of traded notional
      capital: float = 1.0,
  ) -> BacktestResult: ...
  ```
- `BacktestResult` dataclass: daily returns, cumulative returns, turnover, gross/net exposure, per-ticker contribution.
- Helpers in `qfactory/backtest/metrics.py`: `sharpe`, `sortino`, `max_drawdown`, `cagr`, `volatility`, `hit_rate`, `turnover_annualized`. All annualize from daily.
- Tearsheet in `qfactory/backtest/tearsheet.py`: a notebook-friendly function that returns a matplotlib figure with cumulative returns, drawdowns, rolling Sharpe, and a metrics table.
- Tests in `tests/test_backtest.py` covering:
  - A constant-long single asset returns equal the asset's daily returns minus zero costs.
  - A daily-flipping signal incurs the expected cost drag.
  - Sharpe of an iid norma

title: Stand up daily-bar US equities dataset
description:
First dataset: US large-cap equities daily bars + a risk-free rate series.

## Scope

- Pick a free/low-cost source for daily OHLCV. Stooq via `pandas-datareader` or Yahoo via a maintained library is fine for MVP. Document the choice and its known limitations (survivorship bias, splits/dividends handling, free-tier rate limits).
- Universe: current S&P 500 constituents (or a documented subset if rate limits force it). Document how the universe is fetched and snapshotted.
- Pull at least 10 years of daily bars per ticker.
- Adjust for splits and dividends. Store as adjusted prices, with a column noting the adjustment method.
- Risk-free rate: daily 3-month T-bill (DGS3MO from FRED via `pandas-datareader` or similar).
- Persist to `data/lake/` as parquet:
  - `data/lake/equities_us_daily.parquet` (long format: date, ticker, open, high, low, close, adj_close, volume)
  - `data/lake/rf_us_daily.parquet`
- Add a `qfactory/data/loaders.py` with typed functions: `load_equities_daily()`, `load_rf_daily()`.
- Add a `scripts/fetch_data.py` that re-runs the ingestion idempotently.
- Document the dataset (provenance, schema, refresh cadence) in `README.md` or `docs/data.md`.

## Success criter

title: Bootstrap repo and Python toolchain

- `git init` in the project working directory if no repo exists. Make a `main` branch.

- Pin baseline deps: `polars`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `pyarrow`, `pytest`, `ruff`, `pre-commit`, `jupyterlab`, `matplotlib`.
- Configure `ruff` (lint + format) and `pytest` in `pyproject.toml`.
- Wire `pre-commit` with ruff + trailing-whitespace + end-of-file-fixer.
- Repo skeleton: `qfactory/` (package, with `__init__.py`), `tests/`, `notebooks/`, `data/lake/` (gitignored), `posts/`, `scripts/`.
- `README.md` with the public mission statement, the planned roadmap (mirror the QUA-1 plan doc at a high level), and quickstart.
- `.gitignore` for Python + parquet caches + venvs + Jupyter checkpoints.
- A trivial smoke test (`tests/test_smoke.py`) that imports the package and asserts a constant.
- Make a first commit.

## Success criteria

- `uv sync` works from a clean clone.
- `uv run pytest` passes.
- `uv run ruff check .` passes.
- README clearly states what the project is a

title: Notion -> Git nightly mirror sync
description:
Daily 23:00 UTC. Runs the Notion -> Git mirror sync per `infra/notion-sync/RUNBOOK.md`.

Steps when this routine fires:
1. Read `infra/notion-sync/manifest.yaml` (pages where `direction: notion-to-mirror`).
2. For each page, fetch via Notion MCP (`mcp__claude_ai_Notion__notion-fetch`).
3. Write `docs/notion-mirror/<slug>.md` with the standard banner.
4. If `git status` shows no changes under `docs/notion-mirror/`, comment `no-diff: nothing to commit` and close.
5. Otherwise, stage `docs/notion-mirror/`, commit with `docs: nightly Notion sync YYYY-MM-DD`, comment with the commit hash, and close.

Boundaries: never overwrite `docs/ops/`, `lessons-learned/`, `processes/`, or `paperclip-prompts/`. Never sync prompts back to Notion. Never auto-publish.

Provenance: QUA-151 (verification), CEO policy 2026-04-27 (QUA-151 comment 2e2f2b1f). First commit: 9e41a34.

title: Notion -> Git nightly mirror sync
description:
Daily 23:00 UTC. Runs the Notion -> Git mirror sync per `infra/notion-sync/RUNBOOK.md`.

Steps when this routine fires:
1. Read `infra/notion-sync/manifest.yaml` (pages where `direction: notion-to-mirror`).
2. For each page, fetch via Notion MCP (`mcp__claude_ai_Notion__notion-fetch`).
3. Write `docs/notion-mirror/<slug>.md` with the standard banner.
4. If `git status` shows no changes under `docs/notion-mirror/`, comment `no-diff: nothing to commit` and close.
5. Otherwise, stage `docs/notion-mirror/`, commit with `docs: nightly Notion sync YYYY-MM-DD`, comment with the commit hash, and close.

Boundaries: never overwrite `docs/ops/`, `lessons-learned/`, `processes/`, or `paperclip-prompts/`. Never sync prompts back to Notion. Never auto-publish.

Provenance: QUA-151 (verification), CEO policy 2026-04-27 (QUA-151 comment 2e2f2b1f). First commit: 9e41a34.

title: CoS daily rollup wake (CEO dispatch)
description:
Daily CEO wake at 06:00 UTC to dispatch CoS rollup per DL-056. Replaces the QUA-699 rolling-tracker in_progress pattern (blocked 2026-05-02T00:18Z under feedback_inprogress_noop_loop.md).

Each fire spawns a one-shot execution issue assigned to CEO 7795b4b0. CEO reads the issue, creates a CoS subtask (assigneeAgentId 38f933cd) for the rollup deliverable, then closes the parent.

**CoS subtask checklist** (1 fire = 1 deliverable):
1. Roster - list active agents, status, last-run timestamp; flag silent >4h on non-Codex, >12h Codex.
2. Token burn - query last 24h provider-token spend per agent; compare to framework/governance/token_budget.json (DL-056 budget guards). Flag any agent >2x daily envelope.
3. Model fit - flag mismatch between agent task class and adapter model.
4. Findings comment on QUA-699 (history of record).
5. Close the CoS subtask `done`.

**Anomalies between rollups:** CoS is woken by comments on monitored parents (wake_assignee). NOT via continuous in_progress.

Provenance: DL-056 (CoS hire); QUA-699 wake-storm halt 2026-05-02T00:18Z; CEO under DL-017 v2 broadened authority (operational decisions / scheduler windows).

title: QB Monthly Business Review
description: Auto-creates the QB monthly business review issue on the first Monday of each calendar month at 09:00 Europe/Amsterdam. Quality-Business 2 fills the issue using processes/qb_monthly_review_template.md and posts to OWNER + Board. Authorised by OWNER acceptance of QUA-433 interaction f34300d1 on 2026-04-29. First firing: 2026-05-04 09:00 Europe/Amsterdam.

title: CoS daily rollup wake (CEO dispatch)
description:
Daily CEO wake at 06:00 UTC to dispatch CoS rollup per DL-056. Replaces the QUA-699 rolling-tracker in_progress pattern (blocked 2026-05-02T00:18Z under feedback_inprogress_noop_loop.md).

Each fire spawns a one-shot execution issue assigned to CEO 7795b4b0. CEO reads the issue, creates a CoS subtask (assigneeAgentId 38f933cd) for the rollup deliverable, then closes the parent.

**CoS subtask checklist** (1 fire = 1 deliverable):
1. Roster - list active agents, status, last-run timestamp; flag silent >4h on non-Codex, >12h Codex.
2. Token burn - read C:/QM/repo/framework/registry/token_budget.json envelopes; query each agent's spentMonthlyCents; flag any agent >2x daily envelope.
3. Model fit - flag mismatch between agent task class and adapter model.
4. **Findings comment on the CoS subtask itself** (NOT on QUA-699). Subtask is your assigned deliverable; commenting there does not re-trigger wake-storms. QUA-699 remains permanently `blocked` history-of-record; never comment on it. Recurrence #3 of the wake-storm landed 2026-05-05T15:25Z because CoS posted findings on QUA-699; this step is the fix.
5. Close the CoS subtask `done`.

**Anomalies between rollups:** CoS is woken by comm

title: Notion -> Git nightly mirror sync
description:
Daily 23:00 UTC. Runs the Notion -> Git mirror sync per `infra/notion-sync/RUNBOOK.md`.

Steps when this routine fires:
1. Read `infra/notion-sync/manifest.yaml` (pages where `direction: notion-to-mirror`).
2. For each page, fetch via Notion MCP (`mcp__claude_ai_Notion__notion-fetch`).
3. Write `docs/notion-mirror/<slug>.md` with the standard banner.
4. If `git status` shows no changes under `docs/notion-mirror/`, comment `no-diff: nothing to commit` and close.
5. Otherwise, stage `docs/notion-mirror/`, commit with `docs: nightly Notion sync YYYY-MM-DD`, comment with the commit hash, and close.

Boundaries: never overwrite `docs/ops/`, `lessons-learned/`, `processes/`, or `paperclip-prompts/`. Never sync prompts back to Notion. Never auto-publish.

Provenance: QUA-151 (verification), CEO policy 2026-04-27 (QUA-151 comment 2e2f2b1f). First commit: 9e41a34.

title: DEVOPS-008 — Service patch: source-derive + weekend-clone defense (P1)
description:

- Heartbeat `wall_clock_utc` field (per `QUA-11` / earlier QUA-16 work).

## Required deliverable

Patch `D:\QM\mt5\T1\MQL5\Services\Import_DWX_Queue_Service.mq5` `ProcessJob` so that **after** `CustomSymbolCreate` succeeds:

1. Resolve source symbol (same `SOURCE_OVERRIDES` as DEVOPS-006).
2. Read from **source** (broker), not target:

4. **Weekend-clone defense** — if `tick_value` from source reads 0:
   - Option A (preferred): wait + re-read with timeout (e.g. 3 retries × 30

title: DevOps — drop portable.txt marker on T1-T5 (config drift fix)
description:
**DevOps — drop `portable.txt` marker on T1-T5**

Small but real config drift. All factory MT5 terminals were installed with `/portable` flag and are launched portable from scripts, but the `portable.txt` marker file is missing from each install root. Risk: anyone double-clicking a desktop shortcut without the `/portable` argument writes to `%APPDATA%\MetaQuotes\Terminal\<hash>\` instead of the in-place `D:\QM\mt5\T<n>\` data folder. Silent split-brain hard to debug after the fact.

## Acceptance criteria

For each of T1, T2, T3, T4, T5:

1. Drop an empty file at `D:\QM\mt5\T<n>\portable.txt` (zero bytes is fine).
2. Verify the marker file exists post-drop: `Test-Path` returns `True` for all 5 paths.
3. Restart that terminal **from a non-portable path** (e.g. taskbar shortcut without `/portable`) once and confirm it still uses `D:\QM\mt5\T<n>\Bases\Custom\` for custom symbols (no AppData write). Capture evidence path.
4. T6 is **out of scope** — do not touch.

## Evidence to commit

- `infra/scripts/Drop-PortableMarkers.ps1` if you make a script (idempotent: skip if marker exists).
- `D:\QM
eports\ops\devops\portable_marker_evidence_<ts>.json` with per-terminal `pre_check`, `marker

title: SRC02_S02 — chan-bollinger-es: single-symbol M5 Bollinger ±2σ MR on E-mini S&P
description:
## SRC02_S02 — Chan Bollinger ES Mean-Reversion

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-bollinger-es_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-bollinger-es_card.md)
Raw evidence: [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/bollinger_es_inline.md`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/bollinger_es_inline.md)

### Concept (one-liner)

Single-symbol M5 Bollinger-band mean-reversion on E-mini S&P 500 futures. Long when close < MA − 2σ, short when close > MA + 2σ; exit when |close − MA| ≤ 1σ. Lookback unspecified by Chan (defaulted to Bollinger 2001 textbook value of 20 bars; P3 sweep `[10, 15, 20, 30, 50, 100]`).

### Author claims (verbatim, Ch 2 p. 23)

> "If you allow yourself to enter and exit every five minutes, you will find that the Sharpe ratio is about 3 without transaction costs—very excellent indeed! Unfortunately, the Sharpe ratio is reduced to −3 if we subtract 1 basis point as transaction costs, making it a very unprofitable strategy."

**Chan's deliberate transaction-cost FAILURE example #1** (of 3 in SRC02). Pre-cost +3 Sharpe → post-cost −3 at 1 bp/trade. Card drafted per DL-033 Rule 1; **expected pipeline failure at P9b Operational Readiness** once realistic Darwinex spreads are app

title: Paperclip schema: add blocked_reason column + API persistence (DL-046 enforcement gap)
description: Paperclip API exposes blockedReason in issue responses but does not persist it. PATCH /api/issues/{id} silently accepts the field; refetch returns null. Direct DB schema inspection (information_schema on issues table, 38 columns) confirms no blocked_reason / blockedReason / reason column exists. DL-046 requires every blocked state to name the upstream blocker explicitly via this field; the requirement is currently unenforceable at the API/DB layer. Fix: add blocked_reason text column to issues table, wire through API PATCH/GET/list, and update local-boards check tooling to read the persisted field. Until then, blocker reasons live in the comment thread which is brittle for automated scans. Pre-existing audit trail: QUA-662 comment thread shows the reason for blocked status, with verification at /information_schema/columns showing no backing field.

## SRC04_S07 — Lien 20-Day Breakout Trade

**FIRST CANONICAL DONCHIAN-FAMILY CARD ACROSS THE ENTIRE SRC CORPUS.** 3-state machine: ARMED_SCAN (20-day extreme) → ARMED_PULLBACK (2-day opposite extreme within 1 bar) → ARMED_REBREAK (re-break of original 20-day extreme within 3 bars + 5p offset). Initial stop a few pips beyond pullback extreme; TP1=1R partial + BE; 2-bar-extreme trail.

Lien Ch 14 PDF p. 135: 'this type of setup tends to have a very high success rate as it allows traders to enter strong trending markets after weaker players have been flushed out.' Per-trade pip P&L on 3 worked examples (GBPUSD ~+155p, EURUSD ~+414p, AUDUSD ~+182p).

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN
Single-symbol forex spot pair, D1. Reuses `donchian-breakout` flag with card-level `pre-breakout-pullback-required = true` (refinement of canonical Do

title: SRC03_S01 — williams-vol-bo: Volatility Breakout — open ± N% × prior-day range stop-entry, multi-market
description:
## SRC03_S01 — Williams Volatility Breakout

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-vol-bo_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-vol-bo_card.md)
Raw evidence: [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC03/raw/probe_pp15-30.txt`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC03/raw/probe_pp15-30.txt) lines 412-414

### Concept (one-liner)
Williams' calling-card volatility-expansion entry: stop-buy at next-day open + N% × prior-day range; mirror for short. Default N=100; Bonds-context N=30; T-Bonds-best-day N=7.

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "Volatility breakouts. Generally speaking, when the market is 'primed' I will buy at the open the next day +100% of the previous days range. This can be a smaller figure for some markets, but year in and year out it has been very good." (PDF p. 25)

Williams provides NO numeric performance claim for the bare entry. Per BASIS rule, no extrapolated number asserted; pipeline P2-P9 produces actual edge.

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN
Single-symbol, daily bars, multi-market generic. CSR P3.5 validates breadth across Darwinex `.DWX` cohort.
