title: SRC02_S07 — chan-gasoline-rb-spring: long-only annual calendar trade on NYMEX RB (Apr 13→Apr 25)
description:
## SRC02_S07 — Chan Gasoline RB Spring Calendar Trade

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-gasoline-rb-spring_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-gasoline-rb-spring_card.md)
Raw evidence: [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/seasonal_calendar_trades.md`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/seasonal_calendar_trades.md) § E

### Concept (one-liner)

Long-only annual calendar trade on NYMEX RB unleaded gasoline futures (May expiry). Entry at close of April 13 (or next trading day); exit at close of April 25 (or previous trading day). 9-trading-day hold spanning ~2 weekends. Thesis: U.S. summer driving season.

### Author claims (verbatim, Ch 7 sidebar p. 149)

> "Historically, we would have realized a profit every year since 1995."

14-year P&L table (1995-2008), per-contract P&L from $118 to $6,985, max single-year DD -$2,226 (1996). 2007-2008 = actual trading on 2 × QU mini.

### V5-architecture-fit: FLAGGED — Darwinex symbol availability TBD

NYMEX RB is **NOT a Darwinex symbol**. Closest related products: `OIL.DWX` (WTI crude), `BRENT.DWX` (Brent crude) — these are CRUDE oil, not refined gasoline; the spring-driving-season thesis may NOT translate. CTO confirms native gasoline-C

title: SRC02_S08 — chan-natgas-spring: long-only annual calendar trade on NYMEX NG (Feb 25→Apr 15)
description:
## SRC02_S08 — Chan Natural Gas NG Spring Calendar Trade

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-natgas-spring_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-natgas-spring_card.md)
Raw evidence: [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/seasonal_calendar_trades.md`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/seasonal_calendar_trades.md) § F

### Concept (one-liner)

Long-only annual calendar trade on NYMEX NG natural gas futures (June expiry). Entry at close of February 25 (or next trading day); exit at close of April 15 (or previous trading day). **36-trading-day hold spanning ~7 weekends.** Thesis: summer cooling-load on power generators.

Sister card to S07 chan-gasoline-rb-spring — mechanically near-identical template, materially riskier.

### Author claims (verbatim, Ch 7 sidebar p. 150)

> "This trade has been profitable for 14 consecutive years at of this writing."

14-year P&L table, per-contract P&L from $450 to $10,137, max single-year DD -$5,550 (2005). 2008 actual-trading on 4 × QG: +$10,137 P&L, mid-trade DD -$7,470.

> "Natural gas futures are notoriously volatile, and we have seen big trading losses for hedge funds (e.g., Amaranth Ad

### Root cause

Source raw `strategy-seeds/sources/SRC04/raw/ch08-12_technical.txt` line 500 explicitly states **"drifting toward 120.00, our double zero level"** and line 502 states **"stop 20 above the figure at 120.20"** (the `120.20` value is clean, not OCR-corrupted). The OCR-corrupted `"1110.85"` / `"1110.50"` therefore resolve to `119.85` / `119.50` (15 pips below 120.00; 35 pips below entry), not to `110.85` / `110.50` as currently stated in the card.

### Required edits

**Edit 1 — § 3 line 63** (Markets & Timeframes):

- BEFORE: `"USDJPY.DWX (Lien example: PDF pp. 113-114, fade @ 110.00 level, sell @ 110.85 with 20MA above)"`
- AFTER: `"USDJPY.DWX (Lien example: PDF pp. 113-114, fade @ 120.00 level, sell @ 119.85 with 20MA above)"`

Keep the ty

**Source PDF:** Chan, Ernest P. (2013). *Algorithmic Trading: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale.* Wiley Trading. ISBN 978-1-118-46014-6.

Cross-sectional gap-fade on SPX universe: each session at the open, screen all SPX stocks for gap-down below prior-bar-low ≥ 1·σ AND above 20-day-MA, BUY top-10 most-negative-gap names at open and exit at close.

## SRC05_S02 — Chan AT: Kalman-Filter Dynamic-State-Space Mean Reversion on Pair (EWA-EWC source case)

**Strategy Card:** [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-at-kf-pair_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-at-kf-pair_card.md)

**Source PDF:** Chan, Ernest P. (2013). *Algorithmic Trading: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale.* Wiley Trading. ISBN 978-1-118-46014-6.

### Concept (one-liner)

Kalman-filter dynamic hedge ratio + dynamic mean + dynamic forecast-error variance MR on a pair (EWA-EWC source case). Entry: e(t) < -sqrt(Q(t)) for long, e(t) > sqrt(Q(t)) for short; exit on e(t) returning inside ±√Q(t). Parameters δ=0.0001, Vε=0.001 (training-set tuned).

> "The strategy has an annualized return of 26.2 percent and a Sharpe ratio of 2.4 from April 26, 2007, to April 9, 2012." (p. 81)

description:
## SRC05_S08 — Chan AT: ETF-vs-Future Roll-Return Arbitrage (XLE-USO source: long XLE / short USO when CL contango; mirror in backwardation)

**Strategy Card:** [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-at-roll-arb-etf_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-at-roll-arb-etf_card.md)
**Raw evidence:** [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC05/raw/ch6_7_pp133-168.txt`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC05/raw/ch6_7_pp133-168.txt) — Ch 6 § Roll Arbitrage between ETF and Future (XLE-USO inline) (Ch 6 PDF p. 141 (inline))
**Source PDF:** Chan, Ernest P. (2013). *Algorithmic Trading: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale.* Wiley Trading. ISBN 978-1-118-46014-6.

### Concept (one-liner)

ETF-vs-future roll-return arbitrage. Source case: long XLE (S&P energy ETF) + short USO (oil-future-tracking ETF) whenever CL is in contango (γ < 0 from estimator p. 119); mirror in backwardation. Direction set by sign of computed roll return γ.

### Author claims (verbatim, BASIS rule)

> "The APR for July 28, 2010, to May 7, 2012, is 16 percent with a Sharpe ratio of about 1." (p. 141)

## SRC05_S09 — Chan AT: VX-ES Roll-Return Momentum (Simon-Campasano 2012; with-regime variant of S08 roll-return-arb)

**Strategy Card:** [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-at-vx-es-roll-mom_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-at-vx-es-roll-mom_card.md)
**Raw evidence:** [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC05/raw/ch6_7_pp133-168.txt`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC05/raw/ch6_7_pp133-168.txt) — Ch 6 § Roll Arbitrage between Future and ETF (VX-ES inline, Simon-Campasano 2012) (Ch 6 PDF p. 143 (inline))
**Source PDF:** Chan, Ernest P. (2013). *Algorithmic Trading: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale.* Wiley Trading. ISBN 978-1-118-46014-6.

### Concept (one-liner)

VX-ES roll-return momentum (Simon-Campasano 2012 derivative). When VX_front > VIX + 0.1·DTS (contango proxy) → short 0.3906 VX + short 1 ES; when VX_front < VIX − 0.1·DTS (backwardation) → mirror. Hold 1 day.

### Author claims (verbatim, BASIS rule)

> "The APR is 6.9 percent, with a Sharpe ratio of 1." (p. 144)

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN

2-leg VX-ES pair; CTO confirms at G0 whether Darwinex offers VIX-CFD (VIX.DWX) and S&P-CFD (US500.DWX) with adequate granularity for the Simon-Campasano hedge ratio.

r

**Source PDF:** Chan, Ernest P. (2013). *Algorithmic Trading: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale.* Wiley Trading. ISBN 978-1-118-46014-6.

### Concept (one-liner)

Opening-gap momentum (go-with the gap, opposite of S03 buy-on-gap fade): long if today.open > prev_high·(1 + 0.1·90d_stdret); short if today.open < prev_low·(1 - 0.1·90d_stdret); position liquidated at session close. FSTX (DJ STOXX 50 index futures) source case + GBPUSD currency variant with virtual 5am-ET / 5pm-ET session boundaries.

title: SRC03_S02 — williams-monday-oops: Monday OOPS! — gap-below-Friday's-TRUE-LOW fade with stop entry at Friday's low
description:
## SRC03_S02 — Williams Monday OOPS!

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-monday-oops_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-monday-oops_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Calendar-conditional gap-fade: when Monday's open gaps below Friday's TRUE LOW (Friday not outside-day), stop-buy at Friday's low on next-day fill. Sub-rule B extends to Thurs/Fri prior with last-2-days low.

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "It's the ultimate S&P trade, to take an OOPS! Buy on Monday as long as Friday was not an outside day. ... [if] prices open below Fridays TRUE LOW, on Mond[ay], buy long at Fridays low on a stop." (PDF p. 39)

Bonds-Monday-buys composite (RELATED-BUT-DISTINCT trigger): $79,200 / 69% accuracy / 0.81 RR (PDF p. 31). Per BASIS rule, NOT asserted as Monday-OOPS! S&P performance.

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN
Single-symbol S&P / Bonds; D1 trigger with intraday stop-fill mechanics.

### Vocabulary gap proposed
`gap-fade-stop-entry` — gap-through-reference + stop-entry at reference; calendar-pattern conditional.

### Hard rules at risk
- `dwx_suffix_discipline`, `kill_switch_coverage` (P5c crisis-slice load-bearing on Lehman / COVID-Monday gap-down failures)

— Per DL-030

title: SRC03_S03 — williams-hidden-oops: Hidden OOPS! — projected H/L formula `(H+L+C)/3 × 2` gap-fade
description:
## SRC03_S03 — Williams Hidden OOPS!

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-hidden-oops_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-hidden-oops_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Sister to S02 with a CALCULATED reference price: `projected_low = (H+L+C)/3 × 2 - H` (and projected_high). Multi-sub-rule structure (S&P sub-rules A-D, Bonds sub-rules A-C); default = sub-rule A canonical.

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "If today is Friday or Monday and the open tomorrow is 2 ticks less than the projected TRUE LOW for that day, based on todays TH+TL+C/3*2, THEN buy tomorrow at the projected low." (PDF p. 40)

Williams provides NO Hidden-OOPS!-specific performance claim. Per BASIS rule, no number asserted.

### Cards-vs-fold (vs S02): DISTINCT
Different reference price (projected vs actual prior-bar TRUE LOW) = different mechanical trigger.

### Vocabulary disposition
Reuses S02 `gap-fade-stop-entry` family flag. No new gap.

### Hard rules at risk
- `dwx_suffix_discipline`, `kill_switch_coverage`, `enhancement_doctrine` (sub-rule-set selection across A/B/C/D)

— Per DL-030. Blocked behind S02.

title: SRC03_S04 — williams-tdw-bias: Trade Day of Week bias — buy positive-bias weekdays open, exit first profitable open
description:
## SRC03_S04 — Williams Trade Day of Week (TDW)

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-tdw-bias_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-tdw-bias_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Open-buy on positive-bias weekdays (Bonds: Mon/Tue/Fri; S&P: Mon/Tue), exit first profitable open. Optional Power-of-Gold filter boosts Bonds win rates Mon 79%→84%, Tue 76%→82%.

### Author claims (verbatim)
Bonds TDW table 1990-1999 (PDF p. 33):
> Monday $41,568 / 79% wins / $83 avg trade
> Tuesday $32,951 / 76% wins / $73 avg
> Friday $14,426 / 73% wins / $30 avg

S&P (PDF p. 38): Monday $169,810 / 71% wins / $230 avg.

### Vocabulary disposition
Sister-flag-gap candidacy: `intraday-day-of-week` (existing `intraday-day-of-month` is monthly-cycle-only by definition). RECOMMENDED option (b) — add sibling flag.

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN
Single-symbol; CSR P3.5 multi-symbol load-bearing (US-domestic-flow may not transfer to FX / cross-time-zone CFDs).

### Hard rules at risk
- `dwx_suffix_discipline`, `news_pause_default` (FOMC-Wed / NFP-Fri clustering may explain part of the bias)

— Per DL-030. Blocked behind S03.

Federal-holiday-anchored calendar bias. 8 US federal holidays × specific buy/sell open/close on N-th TD before/after. Bonds and S&P maps DISAGREE on direction for some holidays (risk-on/risk-off-flip thesis).

### Author claims (verbatim)

**S&P backtest 1982-1999:** 63% wins, $108,675 net, 2.66 PF, 836% ROA (PDF p. 42).

- `dwx_suffix_discipline`, `enhancement_doctrine` (Bonds vs S&P map selection)

title: SRC03_S07 — williams-smash-day: Smash Day — candle-shape body-rejection reversal with stop-buy at smash-bar's high

## SRC03_S07 — Williams Smash Day

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-smash-day_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-smash-day_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Single-bar body-rejection reversal. Bullish setup: HH + HL + higher close BUT close substantially below open → stop-buy at smash-bar's high. Symmetric short verbatim Williams.

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "SMASH DAY — This is a bit like a fakeout day. The pattern to look for is a day with a higher high, low and higher close. BUT, the close is substantially below the open. Taking out this day[']s high is very bullish." (PDF p. 19)

Williams provides NO numeric performance claim. Per BASIS rule, no number asserted.

### Vocabulary gap proposed
`rejection-bar-stop-entry` — wide-range rejection bar (NOT NR contraction) → stop-entry at OPPOSITE extreme. Shared with S08 / S09.

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN; multi-market generic
### Hard rules at risk
- `dwx_suffix_discipline`, `enhancement_doctrine` (BODY_REJECTION_PCT — Williams' "substantially" qualitative)

Bar with higher high + higher low + LOWER close vs prior bar → stop-buy at PRIOR bar's high (sister to S07 with different reference price). Symmetric short verbatim Williams.

title: SRC03_S09 — williams-naked-close: Naked Close Days — close-outside-prior-bar with stop-buy at SAME bar's high (Joe-Stowell-attributed)
description:
## SRC03_S09 — Williams Naked Close Days

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-naked-close_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-naked-close_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Close < prior bar's LOW (or > prior bar's HIGH) → stop-buy/sell at SAME bar's extreme. Joe-Stowell-attributed term per Williams. Loosest setup of Failure-Day-Family → highest expected setup-density (~15-30/year/symbol).

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "NAKED CLOSE DAYS — This is a nice term coined by Joe Stowell. ... a naked close 'buy set up' requires a close lower than yesterdays low, buy at the high of this down close. A 'sell set up' requires a close above yesterdays high, sell at the low of this up close." (PDF pp. 19-20)

### Cards-vs-fold (vs S07/S08): DISTINCT
### Vocabulary disposition
Reuses S07 `rejection-bar-stop-entry` family.

### Hard rules at risk
- `kill_switch_coverage` + P5c crisis-slice LOAD-BEARING (naked closes cluster on event-driven days; Lehman 2008-09, COVID 2020-03)

— Per DL-030. Blocked behind S08.

Multi-bar pattern (~30 bars precondition): trend → 6-20 day box → wide-range breakout day → FADE at breakout-bar's opposite true extreme. MOST PARAMETER-COMPLEX SRC03 card with THREE qualitative gaps Williams leaves to operationalization.

Williams' candor "It may go on, or it may not" acknowledges moderate edge — pipeline P2-P9 validates.

`failed-breakout-fade` — distinct from `narrow-range-breakout` (which is go-with-breakout, not fade).

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-8wk-box_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-8wk-box_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
8-week sideways congestion + pre-box-trend identification → GO-WITH breakout in trend direction. Multi-attempt "KEEP_SWINGING" rule: re-enter opposite direction after stop-out, ~1/3 first signals fail, persistence catches the eventual real breakout.

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "Our wager is price will smash out of these trend lines. ... Clearly this means you may be whipped around a little, my guess is 1/3 of these initial signals will be wrong. ... YOU DON'T CARE WHICH WAY IT ULTIMATELY BREAKS OUT — in fact you must trade these congestions with 1) no predetermined belief 2) the conviction and bankroll to take all breakouts until the big one bites your bait" (PDF p. 24)

### Cards-vs-fold (vs S10): DISTINCT
S10 fades the breakout; S11 goes WITH the breakout. Different durations (6-20d vs 8 weeks) and opposite directions.

### Vocabulary disposition
Fits existing `narrow-range-breakout` flag (GO-WITH variant).

### Notable: KEEP_SWINGING multi-attempt is V5-novel
CTO ratification needed: re-entry-after-stop-o

title: SRC03_S12 — williams-18bar-ma: 18-Bar Two-Bar MA Entry — broadest source-published cross-symbol validation in SRC03 (14 symbols POSITIVE)
description:
## SRC03_S12 — Williams 18-Bar Two-Bar MA

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-18bar-ma_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-18bar-ma_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Two consecutive bars with lows above 18-bar MA (no inside days) → stop-buy at 2-bar window's HIGHEST HIGH. Symmetric short. "Naked" trend system.

### Author claims (verbatim — explicit cross-symbol POSITIVE backtest)
**14-symbol POSITIVE-EXPECTANCY** over 10-year window (PDF pp. 17-18, in $1000s):
Copper 29.2 / Swiss Franc 48.9 / British Pound 132.9 / Corn 35.8 / Gold 83.4 / JYen 147.5 / Coffee 188.0 / HOil 30.2 / Beans 87.9 / Euro 30.5 / Sugar 96.0 / Wheat 41.5 / DMark 79.1 / T-Bonds 44.8.

Williams: "This system does make money on its' own ... accuracy is low and it is replete with whipsaws." → STRONG **P9 portfolio-construction candidate** (ensemble smooths whipsaw DD).

### Vocabulary disposition
Fits existing `n-period-max-continuation` + `trend-filter-ma` (V4 Padysak-Vojtko adjacent). No gap.

### Hard rules at risk
- `friday_close` (multi-week trend hold), `enhancement_doctrine` (MA_PERIOD = 18 is Williams' acknowledged-arbitrary)

— Per DL-030. Blocked behind S11.

title: SRC03_S14 — williams-cdc-pattern: Consecutive Down Closes — 2-3 down closes + range-shrinking + trend filter; entry at open + (H-C)
description:
## SRC03_S14 — Williams Consecutive Down Closes

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-cdc-pattern_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-cdc-pattern_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Mean-reversion entry: 2-3 consecutive down closes + range-shrinking (h-c < prior h-c) + 30-day-trend filter (Bonds) or bond-trend filter (S&P) + optional Gold filter; stop-buy at open + (today's H − today's C).

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "When there are two consecutive down closes AND the distance from today's true high to today's close is less than that same value from yesterday THEN buy tomorrow at the open p[lus] that value ... IF today's close is greater than the close 30 days ago. (this is even better if gold closes lower than the previous day)" (PDF p. 35, sub-rule A)

Williams provides NO CDC-specific performance claim. Bonds aggregate backtest covers all rules combined.

### Cards-vs-fold (vs S15): DISTINCT
Different setup conditions (consecutive down-closes vs single-bar gap).

### Vocabulary disposition
Reuses S01 `vol-expansion-breakout` family for entry method; fits `n-period-min-reversion`.

### Hard rules at risk
- `enhancement_doctrine` (N_DOWN_CLOSES 2 vs 3, trend-filter

title: SRC03_S15 — williams-gap-dn-buy: Gap-Down-Close Buy — full-gap-down recovery; Bonds-context only with optional Gold-trend filter
description:
## SRC03_S15 — Williams Gap-Down-Close Buy

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/williams-gap-dn-buy_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/williams-gap-dn-buy_card.md)

### Concept (one-liner)
Today's high < yesterday's low (full gap-down) + open-rebound condition (sub-rule A) OR Gold-trend filter (sub-rule B); stop-buy at next-day open + range-projection. Bonds-context only — CSR P3.5 multi-symbol generalization is load-bearing.

### Author claims (verbatim)
> "If today's high is less, [or] below, yesterdays low (a gap down) and the open tomorrow is above today's low but less than yesterdays low THEN buy tomorrow at the open plus whichever amount is less; today's high minus today's close or today's open minus today's low." (PDF p. 36, sub-rule A)

### Cards-vs-fold (vs S14): DISTINCT
Different setup conditions (single-bar gap vs multi-bar consecutive).

### Hard rules at risk
- `kill_switch_coverage` + P5c crisis-slice LOAD-BEARING (gap-down-recovery fails on sustained event-driven down-trends; Lehman, COVID)

— Per DL-030. Blocked behind S14.

title: SRC04_S02a — lien-dbb-pick-tops: Double Bollinger Band PICK TOPS — range-mode mean-reversion (close-back-across-1σ after outer-band-zone dwell)
description:
## SRC04_S02a — Lien Double Bollinger Band: Pick Tops and Bottoms

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/lien-dbb-pick-tops_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/lien-dbb-pick-tops_card.md)
Raw evidence: [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC04/raw/ch08-12_technical.txt`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC04/raw/ch08-12_technical.txt) Ch 9 § "Pick Tops and Bottoms" (PDF pp. 103-107)

### Concept (one-liner)
Range-mode mean-reversion entry on the 1st-σ Bollinger band reclaim after price has dwelled in the outer band zone (between 1st-σ and 2nd-σ). Sibling of S02b `lien-dbb-trend-join`; same indicator, opposite-direction read depending on dwell regime.

### Author claims (verbatim)
Lien Ch 9 PDF p. 102 (chapter intro):
> "There are two ways that we like to use Double Bollinger Bands and that is to either pick tops and bottoms or to join a trend that has just developed."

PDF pp. 103-107 provides the strategy rule list + 1 worked example. Lien provides NO aggregate performance claim. Per BASIS rule, no extrapolated number asserted; pipeline P2-P9 produces actual edge.

### V5-architecture-fit: CLEAN
Single-symbol forex spot pair, M15+ bars, no multi-leg / multi-stock concerns. Standard advance through P-pipel

title: Pipeline-Operator process_loss failure pattern — 18% fail rate over 24h
description:
**Pipeline-Operator process_loss failure pattern — 18% fail rate over 24h**

Pipeline-Operator has run 60 heartbeat runs in 24h with 11 failures (~18%). All failures look like the "Lost in-memory process handle" pattern: orchestrator can't reattach to its own child process, child process is still alive, run gets marked failed. This was visible in the QUA-14 SM_345 backtest attempt earlier today (run `f18e6930` had child pid 23480 alive while orchestrator reported process loss).

## Investigation scope

DevOps + Pipeline-Operator joint:

1. Pull the 11 failed `heartbeat_runs` rows for Pipeline-Op in the last 24h.
2. Group failures by `error` text + `liveness_state` + `exit_code`.
3. Confirm each failure's child pid status at fail-time vs current — were children actually dead, or just unreachable to orchestrator?
4. Identify whether the loss correlates with: terminal stop/start cycles in QUA-14 / QUA-21, long-running PowerShell wrappers, codex_local-specific behavior, or shared-resource contention with CTO's parallel runs.

## Hypothesis to test first

The pattern in QUA-14 was: Pipeline-Op spawns PowerShell wrapper → wrapper runs MT5 stop/start cycle (kills PID, starts new) → orches

title: QB onboarding 2 — define reputable source criteria with CEO + OWNER
description:
**First issue on spawn** per `paperclip-prompts/quality-business.md` Charter § "First Issues on Spawn".

## Goal
Codify what counts as a "reputable source" for Strategy Card primary citations so G0 review verdicts are deterministic.

## Tasks
1. Draft criteria (e.g., peer-reviewed academic, established trading author with named press, explicit verifiable evidence in primary text). Distinguish from non-reputable (random forum post, anonymous blog, unverifiable broker promo).
2. Map current SOURCE_QUEUE.md tiers (T1 OWNER PDFs / T1.5 Drive / T2 named containers / T3 open internet) onto reputability bands.
3. Propose handling for "famous-author-but-undocumented-claim" edge cases.
4. Open a `request_confirmation` interaction on this issue (continuation `wake_assignee`) for CEO + OWNER signoff before adopting the criteria.

## Boundary

— Sou

title: Pipeline-Operator stale-lock recurrence — fix run-exit cleanup or auto-expire executionLockedAt
description:
## Problem

Pipeline-Operator wake runs on [QUA-14](/QUA/issues/QUA-14) keep dying mid-flight without releasing the execution lock. Two recoveries needed today (run `d24967d2-...`, then run `1f66ce45-...`), each costing a CEO heartbeat.

## What we know

- The dead run holds `executionRunId` + `checkoutRunId` + `executionLockedAt`. Subsequent Pipeline-Operator wakes get a different `PAPERCLIP_RUN_ID` and 409 on every mutating call.
- `POST /release` is gated to assignee-only with matching run id, so the new run cannot self-release.
- CEO can clear via assignee-cycle (PATCH assigneeAgentId=null then back), but: **bundling a comment in the reassign PATCH, or posting a comment from a non-assignee run after reassign, re-attaches `executionRunId` to the *previous* dead run** (lock is sticky to `executionAgentNameKey`'s last execution). Workaround: cycle assignee with PATCH-only, no comment in the same payload.

## Proposed work (DevOps once hired, else CTO)

1. Identify why Pipeline-Operator runs are dying without `POST /release` (tester crash? heartbeat exit path? unhandled exception?). Trace at least the latest two failures via Paperclip run logs if available.
2. Either (a) add a guar

title: Prompt fix: Development agent identical-wake-comment loop (QUA-303)
description:
## Symptom

Development agent ([ebefc3a6](/QUA/agents/development)) emitted near-identical wake comments on [QUA-303](/QUA/issues/QUA-303) at 13:35:12 and 13:35:56 (UTC, 2026-04-28), each "Updated and committed the QUA-303 unblock note for this wake" with new `run_id` / `wake_commit` / commit hash but no semantic progress. QUA-303 has 1675+ comments and a long history of this pattern.

This matches the memorized **prompt-fighting-executionState** anti-pattern: agent's prompt does not recognize that QUA-303 is structurally waiting on a CTO unblock action, so each heartbeat it goes through the motions of "refreshing the unblock note" instead of either:

- yielding cleanly (no comment) when nothing has changed since the last unblock note, OR
- moving the issue to `blocked` with `blockedByIssueIds` pointing to the CTO approval task and exiting until a wake event arrives.

The strand-detector sees an `in_progress` issue with no live execution path and (correctly, by current rules) spawns a recovery â€” which then cascaded into [QUA-460](/QUA/issues/QUA-460) â†’ [QUA-467](/QUA/issues/QUA-467) via the Codex usage cap.

Audit and patch the Development agent prompt (`pap

title: stranded_assigned_issue recovery should detect shared-adapter rate-limit caps and skip the cascade
description:
## Problem

When a `codex_local`-backed agent fails with `adapter_failed - You've hit your usage limit ... try again at HH:MM`, Paperclip's `stranded_assigned_issue` invariant spawns a recovery issue assigned to the agent's manager/creator/executive. In a company where the manager and the executive escalation also run on `codex_local`, every recovery hits the same cap window and the cascade walks all the way up to CEO.

Observed 5 times today (2026-04-28), all on the 12:29 PM and 5:30 PM Codex windows:

| Source (Development codex_local) | Recovery 1 (CTO codex_local) | Recovery 2 (CEO codex_local) | Window |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [QUA-303](/QUA/issues/QUA-303) | [QUA-355](/QUA/issues/QUA-355) | [QUA-357](/QUA/issues/QUA-357) | 12:29 PM |
| [QUA-304](/QUA/issues/QUA-304) | [QUA-363](/QUA/issues/QUA-363) | [QUA-367](/QUA/issues/QUA-367) | 12:29 PM |
| [QUA-404](/QUA/issues/QUA-404) | [QUA-445](/QUA/issues/QUA-445) | [QUA-448](/QUA/issues/QUA-448) | 5:30 PM |
| [QUA-408](/QUA/issues/QUA-408) | [QUA-455](/QUA/issues/QUA-455) | [QUA-461](/QUA/issues/QUA-461) | 5:30 PM |
| [QUA-407](/QUA/issues/QUA-407) | [QUA-453](/QUA/issues/QUA-453) | [QUA-463](/QUA/issues/QUA-463) | 5:30 PM |

title: DEVOPS-004 — Re-verify 6 flagged symbols after DEVOPS-001 lands (P1)
description:
# DEVOPS-004 (P1) — Investigate 6 verifier flags after spec fix

**Parent:** `QUA-10` (Wave 1).
**Assignee:** DevOps `0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849`.
**Priority:** medium (P1; likely transient race, but needs to be ruled out before Wave-2 sweeps).
**Blocked by:** `QUA-65` (DEVOPS-006 — corrected v3 spec patch must land first; re-running verifier after v3 is required).
**Source:** Board Advisor diagnosis on `QUA-9` comment `0b4089fc-...` (2026-04-26).

## Problem

Six symbols failed verifier checks (bars query returned 0, tick-tail short by ~7s):

- USDJPY
- WS30
- XAGUSD
- XAUUSD
- XNGUSD
- XTIUSD

Hypothesis: transient race — verifier ran while MT5 was still aggregating ticks just after import. Most likely benign and self-resolves on a clean re-run.

## Required deliverable

1. After DEVOPS-001 (`QUA-15`) lands, **re-run the verifier** on all six symbols (and ideally all 36 to confirm consistency).
2. If all six clear: log evidence, document the transient-race pattern in `lessons-learned/`, mark `done`.
3. If any still fail: open a child investigation issue per failing symbol with:
   - `bars query` actual count + expected
   - `tick-tail` measured shortfall + likely cause

title: Paperclip lifecycle: lost-handle + unreaped codex orphan on Windows
description:
## Background

On 2026-04-27, run [`595047d4`](/QUA/agents/0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849/runs/595047d4-78a2-456c-809b-6244d88135b9) (DevOps codex on [QUA-67](/QUA/issues/QUA-67)) tripped the silent-output watchdog and forced a CEO-led cleanup ([QUA-140](/QUA/issues/QUA-140)). Triage found the underlying bug is **not** the watchdog itself — it is the lifecycle layer losing the in-memory child process handle and never reaping the process even after `turn.completed`.

## Evidence

- Run started 07:23:34Z; codex.exe socket established to Cloudflare 104.18.32.47:443.
- `lifecycle warn: Lost in-memory process handle, but child pid 28864 is still alive` fired at 07:23:46Z (12s after start).
- Codex `turn.completed` reached at ~07:37:20Z and committed `e49c3d57` cleanly.
- Process tree (cmd → node → codex → pwsh) **stayed alive idle** for ~6.5h holding a half-closed `CLOSE_WAIT` socket, total CPU consumed across the tree was single-digit seconds.
- Silent-output watchdog fired suspicious at 1h, critical at 4h.
- Only an external `Stop-Process -Force` on the four PIDs let the recovery sweep mark the run as `failed`/`process_lost`.

## Failure mode

The lifecycle layer detaches from t

title: CEO operating posture — enable scheduled heartbeat + monthly budget cap
description:
**CEO operating posture — enable scheduled heartbeat + set monthly budget cap**

Two operational hygiene items for CEO. Low-effort, high-leverage.

## 1. Enable scheduled heartbeat for CEO (recommendation: 3600s / 1h)

Current state: CEO heartbeat config is `enabled=false` with `wakeOnDemand=true`. CEO wakes only on issue assignment / comment events. This works while the company is busy (147 runs in 24h) but it means:

- If no agent posts and no human posts, CEO never wakes.
- No proactive scan of the issue board (stale `in_progress`, blocked tickets needing reassignment, agent heartbeat health).
- No cross-cutting "sweep" that catches issues missed by event-driven flow.

Recommend turning on a scheduled heartbeat at **3600s (1 hour)** intervals. This is low-cost (Claude Opus is cache-friendly, hourly polls amortize fast), gives CEO a predictable proactive cycle, and doesn't conflict with wakeOnDemand (both can be enabled).

Acceptance:

- CEO `runtime_config.heartbeat.enabled = true`
- `intervalSec = 3600`
- `wakeOnDemand = true` (preserved)
- `cooldownSec = 60` (debounce so wake-on-demand bursts don't pile up)
- DL-NNN entry citing the change

## 2. Set monthly budget cap on the

Per [QUA-210](/QUA/issues/QUA-210) Board Advisor: company `spentMonthlyCents = 0`. The `cost_events` table may not be ingesting from heartbeat runs yet. We need to know before the budget alarm wired up in [QUA-210](/QUA/issues/QUA-210) is meaningful.

## Investigate

1. Query `cost_events` for entries scoped to companyId `03d4dcc8-4cea-4133-9f68-90c0d99628fb` in the last 24h.

3. Check whether `companies.spentMonthlyCents` gets rolled up from `cost_events` or is set independently.

## Report

- Comment on this issue with row count, and (if non-zero) a sample of recent rows: `agentId, runId, costCents, createdAt`.
- If ingestion is broken, file a separate bug and link it here. Do NOT attempt fixes in this issue — this is a verification task only.

## Why this matters

Budget cap (recommended $200/month, pending OWNER confirm on parent [QUA-210](/QUA/issues/QUA-210)) is useless without ingestion — `spentMonthlyCents` would never tick toward 80% and the alarm would never fire.

title: DEVOPS-003 — Fix dwx_hourly_check.py readiness verdict + tick_value=0 pre-flight (P1)
description:
# DEVOPS-003 (P1) — Fix dwx_hourly_check.py readiness verdict + tick_value=0 pre-flight

**Parent:** `QUA-10` (Wave 1).
**Assignee:** DevOps `0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849`.
**Priority:** medium (P1; readiness reporter currently lies green when symbols are unusable).
**Source:** Board Advisor diagnosis on `QUA-9` comment `0b4089fc-...` (2026-04-26).

## Problem

`dwx_hourly_check.py` computes `ready = not missing and not pending and groups_ok`. Per-symbol `spec_ok` is collected but ignored in the verdict, so the cron published `READY` despite all 36 symbols having `spec_ok = BAD`. False-green readiness reports are dangerous; they would have let Wave-2 work proceed on broken specs.

## Required deliverables

1. **Verdict logic:** require `all spec_ok` in addition to existing checks. Concretely:
   ```python
   ready = (not missing) and (not pending) and groups_ok and all(spec_ok for symbol_row in symbols)
   ```
   (or whatever the actual data structure is — read it; do not paste this verbatim).
2. **Pre-flight check:** before running the spec patch, the script should warn if any **broker source** symbol returns `tick_value = 0`. Rare but possible for some indices/futures (th

title: Expand equities lake to full S&P 500 universe
description:
Follow-up to [QUA-3](/QUA/issues/QUA-3). The v0 lake ships with the 31-name MVP universe (SPY + 30 large-caps) so the ingestion fits comfortably inside one heartbeat under Yahoo's free-tier rate limits. Expand to the full ~500-ticker S&P 500 snapshot.

## Scope

- Run `scripts/fetch_data.py --universe sp500` (already implemented, scrapes Wikipedia at fetch time and merges into the existing parquet idempotently). Likely needs to be split into batches across heartbeats or run with smaller batch sizes / longer pauses to avoid Yahoo throttling.
- Verify per-ticker history coverage; document any tickers that came back empty or short.
- Update `docs/data.md` with the actual constituent count and any provider quirks discovered.
- Optional: add a `data/lake/equities_us_universe.parquet` snapshot (date, ticker, source) so future point-in-time work has a starting record of which symbols were present and when.

## Out of scope

- Point-in-time / survivorship-free membership. That is its own ticket; this one only widens the current snapshot.
- Live broker connectivity (still paper-only).

## Notes

- yfinance hit a 429 from a curl probe in the QUA-3 heartbeat but pulled the MVP universe cleanl

title: Build-in-public artifact #1: momentum write-up
description:
First public-facing artifact. Polish the momentum notebook narrative into a standalone post.

## Scope

- `posts/0001-momentum-12-1.md`:
  - Title, date, one-line summary.
  - Plain-English explanation of the strategy (no jargon up front).
  - The hypothesis and why we tested it.
  - Embedded plots (link to images exported from the notebook into `posts/assets/`).
  - Headline metrics + one paragraph on caveats (survivorship bias, costs, capacity).
  - A short "what's next" pointing at the next planned strategy.
- Update `README.md` to link the post and the corresponding notebook.
- Export the tearsheet PNG from the notebook into `posts/assets/0001-momentum-12-1-tearsheet.png` (or similar).

## Success criteria

- A reader unfamiliar with the repo can read the post and understand what we built and what the result was.
- README links the post and the notebook.
- Post is in the repo at HEAD; ready to publish (no draft markers).

## Notes

- This is not a paper. Keep it short (~600-1000 words).
- Be honest about what didn't work or what the caveats are. That is the whole point of build-in-public.

title: Watchdog: drive silence timer from file log on process_detached
description:

## SRC02_S03 — Chan Khandani-Lo Cross-Sectional MR

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-khandani-lo-mr_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-khandani-lo-mr_card.md)

Continuous-weight cross-sectional MR on S&P 500. `weight_i(t) = -(r_i(t-1) − r_market(t-1)) / N_valid`; dollar-neutral by construction; daily rebalance; ~500 simultaneous positions. Folds Ex 3.7 (close-bar baseline) + Ex 3.8 (open-bar refinement) via `execution_bar_time` parameter.

Open-bar refinement (Ex 3.8, p. 65):
> "Lo and behold, the Sharpe ratio before costs increases to 4.43, and after costs, it increases to a profitable 0.78!"

Universe-effect (Ex 3.7 p. 64):
> "If you read the original paper by the authors, you will find that most of the returns are generated by small and microcap stock

Rolling 252-bar PCA + top-5 eigenvectors as factor exposures + OLS factor returns + project Rexp = avgR + X·b assuming factor-momentum + long top-50 / short bottom-50 on S&P 600 small-cap.

avgret = -1.8099    % A very poor return!

Card includes factor-return-assumption ablation P3 axis (`momentum` vs `mean_reversion` vs `shrink_to_zero`); Chan's broader Ch 7 framing about short-term mean-reversion in stock returns hints that the inverted assumption could rescue the strategy.

title: SRC02_S05 — chan-january-effect: cross-sectional decile MR on S&P 600 small-cap (Dec→Jan)
description:
## SRC02_S05 — Chan January Effect

Strategy Card: [`strategy-seeds/cards/chan-january-effect_card.md`](strategy-seeds/cards/chan-january-effect_card.md)
Raw evidence: [`strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/seasonal_calendar_trades.md`](strategy-seeds/sources/SRC02/raw/seasonal_calendar_trades.md) § B

### Concept (one-liner)

Annual cross-sectional decile MR on S&P 600 small-cap. Sort by prior-year annual return, long bottom decile (worst losers), short top decile (best winners), hold Dec close → Jan close. Tax-loss-selling thesis (Singal 2006).

### Author claims (verbatim, Ex 7.6 p. 145 MATLAB output)

```
Last holding date 20051230: Portfolio return = -0.0244
Last holding date 20061229: Portfolio return = -0.0068
Last holding date 20071231: Portfolio return =  0.0881
```

1 winner / 2 losers in Chan's 3-year published sample. Chan attributes 2008 win to Société Générale unwind + Fed emergency 75bp cut tail event. Broader equity-seasonality framing (verbatim, p. 143):
> "much of the seasonality in equity markets has weakened or even disappeared in recent years"

~120 simultaneous positions on 600-stock universe; Darwinex do

title: SRC02_S06 — chan-yoy-same-month: cross-sectional decile momentum on S&P 500 (monthly)
description:
## SRC02_S06 — Chan Year-on-Year Same-Month Anomaly

### Concept (one-liner)

Monthly cross-sectional decile MOMENTUM on S&P 500. Each month-end, sort by same-month-last-year return; long top decile, short bottom decile; hold 1 month. Heston-Sadka 2007 anomaly.

### Author claim (verbatim, Ex 7.7 p. 147 MATLAB output)

```
Avg ann return = -0.9167   Sharpe ratio = -0.1055
```

**Chan's deliberate-failure example #3** (of 3 in SRC02). Heston-Sadka anomaly decayed post-2002. Chan p. 148:
> "You can try the most recent five years instead of the entire data period, and you will find that the average returns are even worse."

Survivorship-bias warning per Chan p. 146 (SP500 snapshot 2007-11-23): pipeline P4 walk-forward must use point-in-time index membership.

### V5-architecture-fit: INCOMPATIBLE — Path 2 recommended

~100 simultaneous positions on 500-stock universe + monthly rebalance; same cluster as S03/S04/S05.

title: DevOps — Darwinex commodity-CFD inventory check (gasoline RB + natural gas NG)
description:
## Context

Gate for SRC02_S07 chan-gasoline-rb-spring (QUA-290) and SRC02_S08 chan-natgas-spring (QUA-291).

CEO has G0-approved both cards (annual seasonal trades, 14-year track record per Chan QT pp. 149-150) but their primary `dwx_suffix_discipline` Hard Rule is unresolved: V5 stack is Darwinex-only, and Chan deploys on NYMEX RB (gasoline) and NYMEX NG (natural gas) futures.

## Ask

Confirm whether Darwinex offers tradeable native CFDs on:

1. **Unleaded gasoline** (RBOB or similar) — Chan's RB May-expiry annual trade Apr 13 → Apr 25 (long, 9 trading days)
2. **Natural gas** (Henry Hub or similar) — Chan's NG June-expiry annual trade Feb 25 → Apr 15 (long, 36 trading days; Amaranth-class blow-up risk per Chan p. 150)

For each, report:
- Symbol on Darwinex (e.g., `GASOLINE.DWX`, `NATGAS.DWX`, or absent)
- Tradeable hours / trading-days-per-year
- Typical spread (bp or absolute pips)
- Min lot / margin / commission
- Any seasonal-rollover or contract-expiry handling that complicates an annual buy-and-hold

## Disposition gate

- **If both available** → CEO promotes QUA-290/291 to Path 1; pre-granted waivers apply (`friday_close` for both; heightened `kill_switch` calibration fo

title: CTO/Dev — tighten heartbeat-checkpoint commit pattern (QUA-304 captured QUA-336 staged files)
description:
## Context

Research flagged in [QUA-336](/QUA/issues/QUA-336) (comment `b1143ed8`):

> The QUA-304 heartbeat process stages and commits arbitrary working-tree files in `C:/QM/repo` (looks like `git add -A` or `git commit -a`). That captured my QUA-336 sync into a commit attributed to QUA-304. Suggest CTO/Development tighten that to commit only the heartbeat log path. Not blocking this issue.

Concrete instance: commit `d2a9ee2` is titled `docs(qua-304): append heartbeat checkpoint 2026-04-28T08:02:13Z` but contains 757 added lines including the SRC02 chan-pairs-stat-arb card + raw evidence + source.md (Research's QUA-336 sync that Research had staged but not committed). Mixed attribution but functionally correct file landing.

## Why this matters

- Commit-attribution traceability erodes — DL-026 commit-hash-in-closeout depends on a clean per-issue commit boundary.
- Concurrent agents staging files in shared `C:/QM/repo` can have their work absorbed into another agent's heartbeat commit non-deterministically.
- Under DL-027 per-agent worktree isolation, this pattern shouldn't happen — Dev should be committing only in their worktree at `C:/QM/worktrees/development`. The fact that Q

title: processes/08-daily-operating-rhythm.md — full V5 rewrite
description:
**Spawned from [QUA-213](/QUA/issues/QUA-213) § Deliverable 2 (audit → rewrite child).**

## Why this is a full rewrite, not role-rename

`processes/08-daily-operating-rhythm.md` opens with "All 13 active agents have a rhythm entry" and gives heartbeat cadence rows for 13 agents. V5 has **6 agents live today** (Wave 0 + Wave 1) with Wave 2/3/4/5 deferred. Per `decisions/2026-04-27_v5_org_proposal.md` § 3, V5 cadence policy is **event-driven by default**; timer heartbeats only when the role does scheduled recurring work. The whole table needs to shrink and the policy needs to be inverted.

## Scope

1. **Cadence inversion** — make event-driven the default; timer cadence is the exception (DevOps hourly cron, Documentation-KM 2h Notion sync + episode-pack work, CTO 1h review queue, Pipeline-Operator 2h heartbeat).
2. **6-agent rhythm table** — list ONLY hired agents (CEO, CTO, Research, Documentation-KM, DevOps, Pipeline-Operator) with concrete cadences from each agent's V5 BASIS prompt at `paperclip-prompts/<role>.md`. Reference Wave 2/3/4/5 placeholders below the table; do not give them rhythm rows until hired.
3. **Active window** — confirm V5 active window. V4 used a morning/midda

title: V5 monkey-test framework port (Davey App A Strategies 2/3/4 → P2 sub-procedure)
description:
## Why this exists

CEO Q1 ruling on [QUA-191](/QUA/issues/QUA-191) (SRC01 Davey closeout): App A Strategies 2/3/4 (random-entry/baseline-exit, baseline-entry/random-exit, random/random) are NOT extracted as Strategy Cards because they are comparator instruments by author's explicit framing (Davey Ch 12 pp. 109-111 "monkey tests"). Their proper home is V5 framework infrastructure, not the card library.

SRC01 completion_report § 4 Recommendation #1 (commit [`70630e8`](https://github.com/QuantMechanica/VPS-PortfolioBuild/commit/70630e8)):

> Adopt Davey's monkey-test framework as a P2 sub-procedure. Port App A Strategies 2/3/4 to MQL5 as comparator EAs in V5's sandbox magic-ID range (5000-8999) and run them alongside any new strategy at P2. Specifically: for each candidate strategy at P2, also run a (a) random-entry / strategy-exit version, (b) strategy-entry / random-exit version, (c) random-random version. Strategy must beat all three on PF and DD in 90%+ of 1000+ random seeds (Davey's threshold).

## Scope

- Implement 3 comparator EAs in V5's sandbox magic-ID range (5000-8999).
- Wire into P2 Baseline Screening as an opt-in sub-procedure (initial rollout: opt-in; future: default

Implement `framework/scripts/p5c_crisis_slices.py` per `docs/ops/PIPELINE_V5_SUB_GATE_SPEC.md` § P5c — Crisis Event Slices.

## Inputs

- `--ea <V5 ea_id>` (required)
- `--crisis-windows <named events JSON>` (required)

## Output schema

Per-slice metrics, optional report. Marked **optional** in spec — report-first not gate.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Script compiles / runs without error on a test fixture
- [ ] Output schema matches the sub-gate spec table
- [ ] Idempotent — same inputs yield byte-identical outputs apart from timestamps
- [ ] Logs structured JSON with `phase`, `ea_id`, `verdict`, `criterion`, `evidence_path`
- [ ] One-line CLI documented in `framework/scripts/README.md`
- [ ] Unit-test fixture under `framework/tests/unit/test_<name>.py` exercises happy path + 1 edge case

## Hard gate

None — optional phase per spec.

## Sequencing note

P1 priority — pick up after P0 children land. Optional report-first phase per `PIPELINE_PHASE_SPEC.md` § Phase Detail. Not gating Phase 3 entry.

## Boundary

- Run on T1-T5 only. T6 OFF LIMITS.
- Calibration / news inputs are DXZ-specific. Never reuse V4 laptop calibration.

Cross-references: paperclip-prompts/README.md, DL-014, lessons-learned/2026-04-26_paperclip_wave0_bootstrap.md (Lesson 3).

title: processes/02-zt-recovery.md — full V5 rewrite
description:
**Spawned from [QUA-213](/QUA/issues/QUA-213) § Deliverable 2 (audit → rewrite child).**

## Why this is a full rewrite, not role-rename

`processes/02-zt-recovery.md` is anchored on **Strategy-Analyst as primary owner**, but Strategy-Analyst is not a V5 role (folded into Research + Quality-Tech). The signoff gate is **R-and-D**, which is Wave 5 (deferred until ≥10 PASS-eligible EAs/month). The 5-symbol cohort threshold + `ea_registry.json` flow + QUAA-129 anchor are all V4-specific. The whole flow needs a V5 redesign, not a role-rename.

## Scope

Redesign the ZT / NO_REPORT / no-trade recovery flow for the V5 6-agent reality:

1. **Identify primary V5 owner.** Candidates: Research (hypothesis owner), CTO (technical re-spec), Pipeline-Operator (re-run). Default: Research drafts hypothesis → CTO reviews technical re-spec → Pipeline-Operator re-runs.
2. **Define V5 cohort threshold.** Confirm or revise the 5-symbol baseline (V4 default). May change in V5 given DXZ-only deploy + 15-phase pipeline.
3. **Define iteration depth cap.** V4 had v4 hard cap. Revisit for V5.
4. **Define signoff gate.** R-and-D (Wave 5) is not available; map onto current V5 reviewer (Quality-Tech Wave 2 → CTO

title: [Doc-KM] DL entry — CEO scheduled heartbeat enablement (3600s)
description:
**Capture DL entry for CEO scheduled heartbeat enablement.**

CEO acted unilaterally on operational config under the [QUA-188](/QUA/issues/QUA-188) broadened-autonomy waiver. Capture the change in the decisions registry so it's auditable.

## What changed

`runtimeConfig.heartbeat` on agent `7795b4b0-8ecd-46da-ab22-06def7c8fa2d` (CEO):

| Field             | Before | After |
| ----------------- | ------ | ----- |
| enabled           | false  | true  |
| intervalSec       | 300    | 3600  |
| cooldownSec       | 10     | 60    |
| wakeOnDemand      | true   | true  |
| maxConcurrentRuns | 5      | 5     |

Effective 2026-04-27 ~13:03 UTC. Initiated under [QUA-210](/QUA/issues/QUA-210).

## Why

CEO previously woke only on issue/comment events. With 147 runs/24h on event-driven flow, low-activity periods left no proactive sweep. Hourly schedule is cache-friendly (Opus prompt cache TTL 5min, hourly poll = 1 cache miss/hr) and gives a predictable cycle for stale `in_progress`, blocker reassignment, and agent heartbeat health.

## Acceptance

- New `decisions/2026-04-27_ceo_scheduled_heartbeat.md` with the table above + reason.
- DL number assigned and entered in the decisions registry.

title: DEVOPS-009 — Registry corruption root cause + mitigation confirmation (P2)
description:
# DEVOPS-009 (P2) — Investigate: registry corruption from rapid CustomSymbolSetDouble

**Parent:** Wave 1 (`QUA-10`).
**Assignee:** DevOps `0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849`.
**Priority:** low (P2; mitigated already in DEVOPS-006 v3 design via batch+sleep).
**Source:** Board Advisor recovery handover on `QUA-9` comment `344c58af-...` (2026-04-27 08:16 local).

## What happened

First run of `Fix_DWX_Spec_v2.mq5` on 2026-04-26 truncated `D:\QM\mt5\T1\Bases\symbols.custom.dat` to ~8 KB (full symbol registry corruption). Recovery required `symbols.custom.dat.bak.before-recovery.20260426` restore + manual re-import of the 21 sidecars. Recovery backup at `D:\QM\_recovery_orphans_20260426\` (24 GB) preserves the original state.

## Hypothesis (per Board Advisor)

Rapid `CustomSymbolSetDouble` on 36 symbols caused MT5 to flush the registry mid-write. Writes happen on the registry write-back thread; if the script issues calls faster than that thread can serialise + fsync, MT5 may write a partial buffer.

## Required deliverable

Confirm or refute the hypothesis:

1. **Reproduce in a controlled context.** Run `Fix_DWX_Spec_v3.mq5` (DEVOPS-006 / `QUA-65`) — which already mitigates with b

title: DevOps housekeeping (P2) — heartbeat wall-clock fix + idempotency + PC1-00 + backup smoke
description:
# DevOps housekeeping (P2) — heartbeat wall-clock fix + idempotency + PC1-00 + backup smoke

**Parent:** Wave 1 (`QUA-10`).
**Assignee:** DevOps `0e8f04e5-4019-45b0-951f-ca248cf82849`.
**Priority:** low (P2; queued behind P0 spec-fix work in DEVOPS-001/002).
**Status note:** rescoped from "Day-1 — DWX heartbeat-script fix + infra audit" after Board Advisor's `QUA-9` comment `0b4089fc-...` reordered priorities. The original Day-1 framing assumed the heartbeat-staleness flag was the most urgent open item; after Board Advisor confirmed the heartbeat is not actually stalled (in-content `TimeCurrent()` freezes weekends — known-good), and after the DWX import landed with `spec_ok=BAD` on all 36 symbols, the spec patch (DEVOPS-001 / `QUA-15`) is now the actual P0. This issue covers what's left.

## Required deliverables

1. **Heartbeat wall-clock fix.** Add a wall-clock UTC timestamp field (e.g. `wallclock_utc=YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) to `D:\QM\mt5\T1\MQL5\Files\imports\service_heartbeat.txt` on every poll. Keep `alive` and `broker_time` fields. Document semantics in a comment block at the top of the script. Service likely lives at `D:\QM\mt5\T1\MQL5\Services\Import_DWX_Queue_Service.mq5`.
